description_plain: Your child can identify different types of taxes and explain why local, state, and federal governments collect them.

description: Identify the components of a personal/family budget (e.g., income, savings/investments, taxes, emergency fund, expenses, and charitable giving) based on short, medium, and long term goals (e.g., financial, personal, educational, and career).
description_plain: Your child can list parts of a personal or family budget based on short, medium, and long-term goals like income, savings, expenses, and giving.

description: Using decision-making models, evaluate how career choices, educational/vocational preparation, skills, and entrepreneurship affect income and standard of living (e.g., postsecondary degree/ certification, needs versus wants, and the ability to live on less than you earn).
description_plain: Your child can use decision-making skills to see how career choices, education, and skills affect income and lifestyle.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to understand the purpose of weather forecasting to prepare for and respond to severe weather.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand why weather forecasts help us prepare for bad weather.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to represent the relationship between the needs of different plants or animals (including humans) and the places they live.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how where plants and animals live helps them get what they need.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by evidence for how plants and animals (including humans) can change the environment to meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using examples, how plants and animals can change their surroundings to get what they need.

subject: Science
description: Use and share observations of local weather conditions to describe patterns over time.
description_plain: Your child can talk about the weather they see around them and notice patterns, like how it changes over time.

subject: Science
description: Use observations to describe patterns of what plants and animals (including humans) need to survive.
description_plain: Your child can notice and describe what plants and animals need to live and grow.

subject: Science
description: Use tools and materials to design and build a structure that will reduce the warming effect of sunlight on an area.
description_plain: Your child can use simple tools and materials to build something that helps keep an area cooler from the sun.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to determine the effect of sunlight on Earth's surface.
description_plain: Your child can observe and notice how sunlight changes the temperature of the ground or other surfaces.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data to determine if a design solution works as intended to change the speed or direction of an object with a push or pull.
description_plain: Your child can look at results to see if their idea for moving something by pushing or pulling works as planned.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to compare the effects of different strengths or different directions of pushes and pulls on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child can test how different pushes or pulls make things move in different ways.

subject: Science
description: Communicate solutions that will reduce the impact of humans on the land, water, air, and/or other living things in the local environment.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas to reduce how people harm the land, water, air, and living things nearby.

subject: Science
description: Make observations at different times of year to relate the amount of daylight and relative temperature to the time of year.
description_plain: Your child can observe changes in daylight and temperature during different times of the year.

subject: Science
description: Use observations of the sun, moon, and stars to describe patterns that can be predicted.
description_plain: Your child can watch the sun, moon, and stars and describe patterns they see, like when and where they appear.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that young plants and animals are like, but not exactly like, their parents.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain how young plants and animals are similar to, but not exactly the same as, their parents.

subject: Science
description: Obtain information from media and/or text to determine patterns in the behavior of parents and offspring that help offspring survive.
description_plain: Your child can find information about how parents and their babies behave to help the babies survive.

subject: Science
description: Use materials to design a solution to a human problem by mimicking how plants and/or animals use their external parts to help them survive, grow, and meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child can use materials to make something that solves a problem by copying how plants or animals use their body parts.

subject: Science
description: Use tools and materials to design and build a device that uses light or sound to solve the problem of communicating over a distance.
description_plain: Your child can use simple tools and materials to make something that uses light or sound to send a message over a distance.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to determine the effect of placing objects made with different materials in the path of a beam of light.
description_plain: Your child will test what happens when different objects are placed in the way of a beam of light.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that objects can be seen only when illuminated.
description_plain: Your child understands that we can only see things when there is light shining on them.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct investigations to provide evidence that vibrating materials can make sound and that sound can make materials vibrate.
description_plain: Your child will do experiments to show that things that vibrate can make sound, and sound can make things vibrate.

subject: Science
description: Obtain information to identify where water is found on Earth and that it can be solid or liquid.
description_plain: Your child can find out where water is found on Earth and that it can be either solid (like ice) or liquid (like water).

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to represent the shapes and kind of land and bodies of water in an area.
description_plain: Your child can create a model showing the shapes of land and water in an area.

subject: Science
description: Compare multiple solutions designed to slow or prevent wind or water from changing the shape of the land.
description_plain: Your child can compare different ways to stop wind or water from changing the shape of the land.

subject: Science
description: Use information from several sources to provide evidence that Earth events can occur quickly or slowly.
description_plain: Your child can use information from different sources to show that Earth events can happen quickly or slowly.

subject: Science
description: Make observations of plants and animals to compare the diversity of life in different habitats.
description_plain: Your child can look at plants and animals in different places and compare how many different kinds there are.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple model that mimics the function of an animal in dispersing seeds or pollinating plants.
description_plain: Your child can make a simple model that shows how animals help move seeds or pollinate plants.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to determine if plants need sunlight and water to grow.
description_plain: Your child can do an experiment to find out if plants need sunlight and water to grow.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that some changes caused by heating or cooling can be reversed and some cannot.
description_plain: Your child can explain, with examples, that some changes from heating or cooling can be undone, but others cannot.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account of how an object made of a small set of pieces can be disassembled and made into a new object.
description_plain: Your child can show how something made from a few pieces can be taken apart and used to make something new.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data obtained from testing different materials to determine which materials have the properties that are best suited for the intended purpose.
description_plain: Your child can test different materials to find out which ones are best for a certain job.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to describe and classify different kinds of materials by their observable properties.
description_plain: Your child can sort and describe different materials by looking at how they look, feel, or act.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a design solution that reduces the impacts of a weather-related hazard.
description_plain: Your child can explain why a design helps reduce problems caused by weather hazards.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe climates in different regions of the world.
description_plain: Your child can gather and combine information to describe what the weather is like in different parts of the world.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in tables and graphical displays to describe typical weather conditions expected during a particular season.
description_plain: Your child can use tables or graphs to show what the weather is usually like during a certain season.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a solution to a problem caused when the environment changes and the types of plants and animals that live there may change.
description_plain: Your child can explain why a solution to a problem caused by environmental changes is helpful.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that in a particular habitat some organisms can survive well, some survive less well, and some cannot survive at all.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to show that some living things do well, some do okay, and some can't survive at all in certain places.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation for how the variations in characteristics among individuals of the same species may provide advantages in surviving and reproducing.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how differences between animals or plants of the same kind can help them survive, find mates, or have babies.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from fossils to provide evidence of the organisms and the environments in which they lived long ago.
description_plain: Your child can look at fossil data to learn about what kinds of living things and environments existed long ago.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to support the explanation that traits can be influenced by the environment.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to show that things like weather or food can affect the traits of plants and animals.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence that plants and animals have traits inherited from parents and that variation of these traits exists in a group of similar organisms.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to show that plants and animals get traits from their parents, and that there are differences even within the same kind.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that some animals form groups that help members survive.
description_plain: Your child can explain why some animals live in groups to help each other survive.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use models to describe that organisms have unique and diverse life cycles but all have a common pattern of birth, growth, reproduction, and death.
description_plain: Your child can make and use models to show that all living things grow, reproduce, and die, even though their life cycles are different.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem that can be solved by applying scientific ideas about magnets.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple problem that can be solved by using what they know about magnets.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to determine cause and effect relationships of electric or magnetic interactions between two objects not in contact with each other.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to figure out how electricity or magnets can make things happen between objects that aren't touching.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements of an object's motion to provide evidence that a pattern can be used to predict future motion.
description_plain: Your child can watch how something moves and notice patterns to help predict how it will move next.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct investigations on the effects of balanced and unbalanced forces on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do experiments to see how balanced and unbalanced forces affect how things move.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions to reduce the impacts of natural Earth processes on humans.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare different ways to protect people from natural events like floods or earthquakes.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe that energy and fuels are derived from renewable and non-renewable resources and how their uses affect the environment.
description_plain: Your child can gather and combine information to explain where energy and fuels come from and how using them affects the environment.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from maps to describe patterns of Earth's features.
description_plain: Your child can look at maps and find patterns in Earth's features, like mountains or rivers.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct investigations on the effects of water, ice, wind, and vegetation on the relative rate of weathering and erosion.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do experiments to see how water, ice, wind, and plants change the speed of weathering and erosion.

subject: Science
description: Identify evidence from patterns in rock formations and fossils in rock layers to support an explanation for changes in a landscape over time.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from rocks and fossils to explain how landscapes have changed over time.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to describe that animals receive different types of information through their senses, process the information in their brain, and respond to the information in different ways.
description_plain: Your child can show that animals use their senses to get information, think about it, and respond in different ways.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that plants and animals have internal and external structures that function to support survival, growth, behavior, and reproduction.
description_plain: Your child can explain that plants and animals have body parts inside and outside that help them survive, grow, act, and have babies.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions that use patterns to transfer information.
description_plain: Your child can create and compare different ways to use patterns to send information.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe that light reflecting from objects and entering the eye allows objects to be seen.
description_plain: Your child can show that we see things because light bounces off objects and enters our eyes.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model of waves to describe patterns in terms of amplitude and wavelength, and to show that waves can cause objects to move.
description_plain: Your child can create and use a model of waves to show patterns like height and length and explain how waves can move objects.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific ideas to design, test, and refine a device that converts energy from one form to another.
description_plain: Your child can use science ideas to design, test, and improve a device that changes energy from one form to another.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions and predict outcomes about the changes in energy that occur when objects collide.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions and guess what will happen to energy when things bump into each other.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to provide evidence that energy can be transferred from place to place by sound, light, heat, and electric currents.
description_plain: Your child can observe and show that energy can move from place to place through sound, light, heat, or electricity.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation relating the speed of an object to the energy of that object.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain how the speed of something is related to how much energy it has.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information about ways individual communities use science ideas to protect the Earth's resources and environments.
description_plain: Your child can find and share ways that communities use science to protect Earth's resources and environment.

subject: Science
description: Describe and graph amounts of saltwater and freshwater in various reservoirs to provide evidence about the distribution of water on Earth.
description_plain: Your child can describe and make graphs showing how much saltwater and freshwater are in different places on Earth to understand where water is found.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe ways the geosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere, and/or atmosphere interact.
description_plain: Your child will create a model to show how the land, living things, water, and air work together.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in graphical displays to reveal patterns of daily changes in the length and direction of shadows, in addition to different positions of the sun, moon, and stars at different times of the day, month, and year.
description_plain: Your child can make graphs to show how shadows change during the day and how the sun, moon, and stars move at different times.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument with evidence that differences in the apparent brightness of the sun compared to other stars is due to their relative distances from Earth.
description_plain: Your child understands and can explain that the sun looks brighter than other stars because it is closer to Earth.

subject: Science
description: Use models to explain factors that upset the stability to local ecosystems.
description_plain: Your child can use models to explain what things can disturb or change a local ecosystem.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe the movement of matter among plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment.
description_plain: Your child will create a model to show how matter moves between plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that plants get the materials they need for growth chiefly from air and water.
description_plain: Your child can explain that plants mainly get what they need to grow from air and water.

subject: Science
description: Use models to describe that energy in animals' food (used for body repair, growth, motion, and to maintain body warmth) was once energy from the sun.
description_plain: Your child can show that the energy animals get from food originally comes from the sun.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument, with evidence, that Earth's gravitational force pulls objects downward toward the center of the earth.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence that Earth's gravity pulls things down toward its center.

subject: Science
description: Conduct an investigation to determine whether the mixing of two or more substances results in new substances.
description_plain: Your child can test what happens when different substances are mixed to see if new substances are made.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and measurements to identify materials based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can observe and measure things to figure out what materials they are made of.

subject: Science
description: Measure and graph quantities to provide evidence that regardless of the type of change that occurs when heating, cooling, or mixing substances, the total weight of matter is conserved.
description_plain: Your child can measure and graph the weight of materials before and after changes to show that the total weight stays the same.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe that matter is made of particles too small to be seen.
description_plain: Your child can show that all matter is made of tiny particles that are too small to see.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data on natural hazards to forecast future catastrophic events and inform the development of technologies to mitigate their effects.
description_plain: Your child can study data about natural disasters to predict future events and help create ways to reduce their damage.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe how unequal heating and rotation of the Earth causes patterns of atmospheric and oceanic circulation that determine regional climates.
description_plain: Your child will build and use a model to show how uneven heating and Earth's spinning cause air and ocean movements that affect local climates.

subject: Science
description: Collect data to provide evidence for how the motions and complex interactions of air masses result in changes in weather conditions.
description_plain: Your child can collect data to show how moving air masses interact and cause changes in the weather.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe the cycling of water through Earth's systems driven by energy from the sun and the force of gravity.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to explain how water moves through Earth's systems because of the sun's energy and gravity.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data on the patterns of distribution of fossils and rocks, continental shapes, and seafloor structures to provide evidence of the past plate motions.
description_plain: Your child can study fossils, rocks, continents, and ocean floors to find evidence of how Earth's plates have moved in the past.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation based on evidence for how geoscience processes have changed Earth's surface at varying time and spatial scales.
description_plain: Your child can explain how Earth's surface has changed over time by using evidence from Earth science.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe the cycling of Earth's materials and the flow of energy that drives these processes within and among Earth's systems.
description_plain: Your child will create a model to show how Earth's materials cycle and how energy moves through Earth's systems.

subject: Science
description: Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence from rock strata for how the geologic time scale is used to organize Earth's geologic history.
description_plain: Your child can explain how scientists use layers of rock to organize Earth's history over long periods.

subject: Science
description: Gather and synthesize information that sensory receptors respond to stimuli by sending messages to the brain for immediate behavior or storage as memories.
description_plain: Your child understands that senses send messages to the brain, which can cause quick actions or become memories.

subject: Science
description: Use an argument supported by evidence for how the body is a system of interacting subsystems composed of groups of cells.
description_plain: Your child understands and can explain that the body is made of systems working together, and these systems are made of groups of cells.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe the function of a cell as a whole and ways parts of cells contribute to the function.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how a cell works and how its parts help it function.

subject: Science
description: Conduct an investigation to provide evidence that living things are made of cells; either one cell or many different numbers and types of cells.
description_plain: Your child can investigate and show that all living things are made of cells, either just one or many different types.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe that waves are reflected, absorbed, or transmitted through various materials.
description_plain: Your child will create and use a model to show how waves can bounce off, be absorbed by, or pass through different materials.

subject: Science
description: Plan an investigation to determine the relationships among the energy transferred, the type of matter, the mass, and the change in the average kinetic energy of the particles as measured by the temperature of the sample.
description_plain: Your child can plan experiments to see how energy, type of material, mass, and temperature are related.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific principles to design, construct, and test a device that either minimizes or maximizes thermal energy transfer.
description_plain: Your child can design and test a device to control how heat moves in or out.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model that predicts and describes changes in particle motion, temperature, and state of a pure substance when thermal energy is added or removed.
description_plain: Your child can make a model that predicts how particles move, temperature changes, and states of matter change when heat is added or taken away.

subject: Science
description: Obtain, evaluate, and communicate evidence of the factors that have caused changes in global temperatures over the past century.
description_plain: Your child can find, check, and share evidence about what has caused global temperatures to change over the last 100 years.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by evidence for how increases in human population and per-capita consumption of natural resources impact Earth's systems.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence how more people and using more natural resources affect Earth's systems.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific principles to design a method for monitoring and minimizing human impact on the environment.
description_plain: Your child can use science to design ways to watch and reduce how humans impact the environment.

subject: Science
description: Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence for how the uneven distributions of Earth's mineral, energy, and groundwater resources are the result of past and current geoscience processes.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how Earth's minerals, energy, and water are spread out unevenly because of natural processes that happened in the past and are still happening today.

subject: Science
description: Evaluate competing design solutions for maintaining biodiversity and ecosystem services.
description_plain: Your child can compare different ideas for keeping plants and animals diverse and ecosystems healthy.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by empirical evidence that changes to physical or biological components of an ecosystem affect populations.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to argue that changes in an ecosystem's parts can affect the living things there.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe the cycling of matter and flow of energy among living and nonliving parts of an ecosystem.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how matter and energy move between living things and their environment.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation that predicts patterns of interactions among organisms across multiple ecosystems.
description_plain: Your child can explain and predict how different living things interact in various ecosystems.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence for the effects of resource availability on organisms and populations of organisms in an ecosystem.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to show how the amount of resources affects living things in an ecosystem.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe how food molecules in plants and animals are broken down and rearranged through chemical reactions to form new molecules that support growth and/or release energy as matter moves through an organism.
description_plain: Your child will create a model to show how food molecules in plants and animals are broken down and rearranged to help growth or release energy.

subject: Science
description: Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence for the role of photosynthesis in the cycling of matter and flow of energy into and out of organisms.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how photosynthesis helps move matter and energy into and out of living things.

subject: Science
description: Construct, use, and present arguments to support the claim that when the kinetic energy of an object changes, energy is transferred to or from the object.
description_plain: Your child can make and share arguments to show that when an object's movement energy changes, energy moves to or from that object.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe that when objects interacting at a distance change their arrangement, different amounts of potential energy are stored in the system.
description_plain: Your child will create a model to show that when objects that affect each other from a distance move, the stored energy changes.

subject: Science
description: Construct and interpret graphical displays of data to describe the proportional relationships of kinetic energy to the mass of an object and to the speed of an object.
description_plain: Your child can make and read graphs to show how an object's movement energy relates to its mass and speed.

subject: Science
description: Construct, test, and modify a device that releases or absorbs thermal energy by chemical processes to solve a problem.
description_plain: Your child can build, test, and improve a device that uses chemical reactions to give off or take in heat to solve a problem.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe how the total number of atoms does not change in a chemical reaction and thus mass is conserved.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show that the number of atoms stays the same in a chemical reaction, so mass is not lost or gained.

subject: Science
description: Gather and make sense of information to describe that synthetic materials come from natural resources and impact society.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain information showing that man-made materials come from natural resources and affect our lives.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret patterns of data related to the properties of substances before and after the substances interact to determine if a chemical reaction has occurred.
description_plain: Your child can study data about substances before and after they react to decide if a chemical reaction happened.

subject: Science
description: Develop models to describe the atomic composition of simple molecules and extended structures.
description_plain: Your child can use models to show what atoms and molecules look like in simple and more complex substances.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to determine scale properties of objects in the solar system.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to compare the sizes and distances of objects in our solar system.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe the role of gravity in the motions within galaxies and the solar system.
description_plain: Your child can make a model to show how gravity affects the movement of stars, planets, and other objects in space.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model of the Earth-sun-moon system to describe the cyclic patterns of lunar phases, eclipses of the sun and moon, and seasons.
description_plain: Your child can make a model of the Earth, sun, and moon to show why we have moon phases, eclipses, and seasons.

subject: Science
description: Use mathematical representations to support explanations of how natural selection may lead to increases and decreases of specific traits in populations over time.
description_plain: Your child can use math to explain how natural selection can cause certain traits in a group of living things to become more or less common over time.

subject: Science
description: Gather and synthesize information about the practices that have changed the way humans influence the inheritance of desired traits in organisms.
description_plain: Your child can gather and combine information about how humans have changed how traits are passed on in living things.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation based on evidence that describes how genetic variations of traits in a population increase some individuals' probability of surviving and reproducing in a specific environment.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how differences in traits help some living things survive and have babies in certain environments.

subject: Science
description: Analyze displays of pictorial data to compare patterns of similarities in the embryological development across multiple species to identify relationships not evident in the fully formed anatomy.
description_plain: Your child can look at pictures of embryos from different species to find patterns and relationships that aren't obvious in fully grown animals.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific ideas to construct an explanation for the patterns of anatomical similarities and differences among modern organisms and between modern and fossil organisms to infer ancestral relationships.
description_plain: Your child can use science ideas to explain why animals and plants today and in fossils have similarities and differences that show family relationships.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to identify patterns within the fossil record that document the existence, diversity, extinction, and change of life forms throughout the history of life on Earth.
description_plain: Your child can study and explain patterns in fossils that show how life has existed, changed, and sometimes disappeared over Earth's history.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe why asexual reproduction results in offspring with identical genetic information and sexual reproduction results in offspring with genetic variation.
description_plain: Your child will create and use a model to explain why asexual reproduction makes identical offspring and sexual reproduction makes varied offspring.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe why structural changes to genes (mutations) located on chromosomes may affect proteins and may result in harmful, beneficial, or neutral effects to the structure and function of the organism.
description_plain: Your child can make a model to show how changes in genes (mutations) can affect proteins and may help, harm, or not change an organism.

subject: Science
description: Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence for how environmental and genetic factors influence the growth of organisms.
description_plain: Your child can explain how both the environment and genes affect how living things grow.

subject: Science
description: Use arguments based on empirical evidence and scientific reasoning to support an explanation for how characteristic animal behaviors and specialized plant structures affect the probability of successful reproduction of animals and plants respectively.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence and reasoning to explain how animal behaviors and special plant parts help them reproduce successfully.

subject: Science
description: Integrate qualitative scientific and technical information to support the claim that digitized signals (sent as wave pulses) are a more reliable way to encode and transmit information.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using information, why digital signals (like computer data) are more reliable for sending information than older, analog signals.

subject: Science
description: Use mathematical representations to describe patterns in a simple model for waves that includes how the amplitude of a wave is related to the energy in a wave.
description_plain: Your child can use math to describe wave patterns and how wave height relates to the energy in the wave.

subject: Science
description: Conduct an investigation and evaluate the experimental design to provide evidence that fields exist between objects exerting forces on each other even though the objects are not in contact.
description_plain: Your child can do experiments to show that objects can push or pull on each other even when they are not touching.

subject: Science
description: Construct and present arguments using evidence to support the claim that gravitational interactions are attractive and depend on the masses of interacting objects.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain that gravity pulls objects toward each other and that the strength depends on how much mass the objects have.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions about data to determine the factors that affect the strength of electric and magnetic forces.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions about data to find out what affects how strong electric and magnetic forces are.

subject: Science
description: Plan an investigation to provide evidence that the change in an object's motion depends on the sum of the forces on the object and the mass of the object.
description_plain: Your child can plan and carry out an experiment to show that how an object moves depends on the total forces acting on it and its mass.

subject: Science
description: Apply Newton's Third Law to design a solution to a problem involving the motion of two colliding objects in a system.
description_plain: Your child can use Newton's Third Law (for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction) to solve problems about two objects bumping into each other.

subject: Science
description: Evaluate design solutions for a major global or local environmental problem that reduces or stabilizes the impacts of human activities on natural systems.
description_plain: Your child can evaluate different solutions to big environmental problems that reduce or keep steady the effects of human actions on nature.

description_plain: Your child can use computer simulations to show how natural resources, people, and biodiversity change and affect Earth's systems.

description: Evaluate competing design solutions for developing, managing, and utilizing energy and mineral resources based on cost benefit ratios on large and small scales.
description_plain: Your child can compare different plans for using energy and mineral resources by looking at their costs and benefits on big and small scales.

description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence how natural resources, natural disasters, and climate changes affect human activities.

description_plain: Your child can argue using evidence how Earth's systems and life evolved together, causing times of stability and change over Earth's history.

description_plain: Your child will create a detailed model to show how carbon moves through water, air, land, and living things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express their ideas using a combination of drawing and emergent writing with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can share their ideas by drawing and beginning to write, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listen and respond to read-alouds, participate in shared reading, and interact independently with texts.
description_plain: Your child listens to stories read aloud, joins in shared reading, and looks at books on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will read and write independently for a variety of purposes and periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can read and write on their own for different reasons and for different amounts of time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Combine movement with relevant props, images, or illustrations to support their writing and speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use movement and related props or pictures to help tell their stories or share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explore ideas and topics in multimodal content.
description_plain: Your child explores ideas and topics using different types of media like pictures, sounds, and videos.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will comprehend and communicate knowledge through alphabetic, aural, visual, spatial, and/or gestural content.
description_plain: Your child can understand and share ideas using letters, sounds, pictures, space, and gestures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Share relevant information for various purposes with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can share information for different reasons with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Find information from provided sources during group research with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can find information from given sources during group research with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generate topics of interest and decide if a friend, teacher, or expert can answer their questions with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can think of topics they like and decide if a friend, teacher, or expert can answer their questions, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Identify and use graphic and text features to understand texts:
  • photos
  • illustrations
  • titles
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, illustrations, and titles to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify relevant pictures, charts, grade-level texts, or people as sources of information on a topic of interest.
description_plain: Your child can find pictures, charts, books, or people that give information about a topic they are interested in.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will engage in inquiry to acquire, refine, and communicate accurate information.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, find accurate information, and share what they learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and begin to use periods, question marks, and exclamation points.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and start using periods, question marks, and exclamation points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize their first name and the pronoun I with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can capitalize their first name and the word I with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to compose simple sentences that begin with a capital letter and conclude with an end mark.
description_plain: Your child can begin writing simple sentences that start with a capital letter and end with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Recognize parts of speech in sentences with prompting:
  • concrete objects as persons, places, or things (i.e., nouns)
  • words as actions (i.e., verbs)
  • color and size adjectives
  • the pronoun I
  • spatial and time relationships such as up , down , before , and after
description_plain: Your child can recognize nouns (people, places, things), verbs (actions), adjectives (describing words), the pronoun I, and words that show position or time, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize simple sentences with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can recognize simple sentences with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will expand and apply knowledge of grammar, usage, mechanics, and style to comprehend texts and communicate effectively.
description_plain: Your child can use grammar, punctuation, and writing style to understand what they read and to communicate clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language according to purpose in shared writing experiences.
description_plain: Your child can use language for different purposes during shared writing activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use new vocabulary to produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child can use new words to make and add to complete sentences during group language activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to acquire new vocabulary and relate new words to prior knowledge.
description_plain: Your child begins to learn new words and connect them to what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to develop an awareness of context clues through read-alouds and other text experiences.
description_plain: Your child starts to notice clues in stories and texts that help understand the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Name and sort familiar objects into categories based on similarities and differences with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can name and sort familiar objects into groups based on how they are alike or different, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will expand and apply their spoken and reading vocabularies to speak, read, and write effectively.
description_plain: Your child can grow and use their speaking and reading vocabulary to talk, read, and write well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use drawing, labeling, and writing to tell a story, share information, or express an opinion with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, labeling, and writing to tell a story, share information, or say what they think, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters and setting in a story with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters and where a story takes place, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer basic questions (e.g., who, what, where, and when) about texts, photographs, or illustrations during shared reading or other text experiences with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer simple questions like who, what, where, and when about stories or pictures during shared reading, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the roles of an author and illustrator in various texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain what authors and illustrators do in different books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will apply critical thinking skills to reading and writing.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully about what they read and write to understand and explain ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Share their stories/texts with authentic audiences.
description_plain: Your child will share their stories or writing with real listeners or readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add to their drawing and emergent writing.
description_plain: Your child can add more details to their drawings and early writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate spacing between letters and words in emergent writing.
description_plain: Your child will leave proper spaces between letters and words when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sequence the action or details of stories/texts with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can put the events or details of a story in order with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express themselves through drawing and emergent writing.
description_plain: Your child expresses ideas by drawing pictures and beginning to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to retell facts and details from an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can start to retell facts and details from an informational book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sequence the plot (i.e., beginning, middle, and end) of a story with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can tell the beginning, middle, and end of a story with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discriminate between fiction and nonfiction text with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between made-up stories and real information with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the topic or main idea with a supporting detail of a text with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea and one detail from a story or text with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will use a variety of recursive reading and writing processes.
description_plain: Your child uses different steps over and over to read and write better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Represent all of the sounds in a Consonant/Vowel/Consonant (CVC) word logically in phonetic spelling (e.g., "kat" for cat or "fer" for fur ).
description_plain: Your child can spell simple words by writing the sounds they hear, like 'kat' for cat.

subject: Language Arts
description: Represent major consonants logically in phonetic spelling (e.g., "lefnt" for elephant or "apl" for apple ).
description_plain: Your child can spell words by writing the main consonant sounds they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to develop a sight word vocabulary by decoding and reading regularly- and irregularly-spelled words in isolation and context with increasing automaticity.
description_plain: Your child is starting to recognize and read common words quickly, even if they are spelled in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recite familiar texts (e.g., rhymes, songs, poetry, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can say aloud familiar rhymes, songs, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read their first and last name in print.
description_plain: Your child can read their first and last name in print.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend letter sounds to decode simple Vowel/Consonant (VC) and Consonant/Vowel/Consonant (CVC) words (e.g., VC words = at , in , up ; CVC words = pat , hen , lot ).
description_plain: Your child can blend letter sounds to read simple words like 'at' and 'pat'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce the sounds for short and long vowels and the most common sound for consonants (e.g., a = /ă/ & /ā/, f = /f/, s = /s/, x = /ks/).
description_plain: Your child can say the sounds for short and long vowels and common consonants.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sequence the letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can put the letters of the alphabet in the correct order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Name all uppercase and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can name all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly form letters to write their first and last name and most uppercase and lowercase letters correctly.
description_plain: Your child can write their first and last name and most letters correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., capitalization of the first word, ending punctuation: period, exclamation point, question mark) with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can recognize that sentences start with a capital letter and end with punctuation like a period or question mark, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that written words are made up of letters and are separated by spaces.
description_plain: Your child knows that words are made of letters and are separated by spaces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that print moves from top to bottom, left to right, and front to back.
description_plain: Your child understands that we read print from top to bottom and left to right.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate correct book orientation and identify the title, title page, and the front and back covers of a book.
description_plain: Your child can hold a book the right way and find the title, title page, and front and back covers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate their understanding that print carries a message by recognizing labels, signs, and other print in the environment.
description_plain: Your child knows that print carries meaning by recognizing signs, labels, and words around them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment phonemes in one-syllable spoken words with 2-4 phonemes (e.g., flat = /f/ /l/ /a/ /t/).
description_plain: Your child can break apart the sounds in short spoken words with 2 to 4 sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend phonemes to form one-syllable spoken words with 2-4 phonemes (e.g., /f/ /l/ /a/ /t/= flat ).
description_plain: Your child can put together sounds to say short spoken words with 2 to 4 sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onset and rime in one-syllable spoken words (e.g., blending: /j/ + og = jog ; segmenting: cat = /c/+ at).
description_plain: Your child can blend and break apart the first sound and the rest of a word in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, segment, and blend syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count, break apart, and blend the parts of words called syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial and final sounds in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and say the first and last sounds in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish rhyming and non-rhyming pairs and produce a rhyme when given a word.
description_plain: Your child can tell which words rhyme and make a rhyme when given a word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count one-syllable and multisyllabic spoken words in a sentence (e.g., I see an elephant.).
description_plain: Your child can count how many words are in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will develop foundational skills for reading and writing proficiency by working with sounds, letters, and text.
description_plain: Your child builds important reading and writing skills by working with sounds, letters, and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally describe personal interests or tell stories to peers and adults using agreed-upon rules (e.g., taking turns, staying on topic).
description_plain: Your child can talk about their interests or tell stories to others while taking turns and staying on topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer relevant questions with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about topics with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about various topics and texts, including their own writing, with peers and adults in small and large groups with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can join group talks about different topics and their own writing with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work respectfully with peers with prompting.
description_plain: Your child learns to work nicely with other children with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow simple one- and two-step oral directions.
description_plain: Your child can follow simple one- or two-step spoken instructions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively listen using agreed-upon discussion rules with prompting.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully and follows agreed rules during discussions with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will listen and speak effectively in a variety of situations.
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and speak clearly in different situations.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how various community members including police officers, firefighters, soldiers, school personnel, business professionals, and medical personnel impact the student's life.
description_plain: Your child understands how community helpers like police officers, firefighters, soldiers, teachers, business people, and doctors help in everyday life.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify ways that people use their money, including spending and saving.
description_plain: Your child can name ways people use money, like spending it or saving it.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the relationship between work and earning money.
description_plain: Your child knows that people work to earn money.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the basic needs of all people: food, clothing, and shelter; differentiate between these needs and a want.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between things people need, like food, clothes, and shelter, and things they just want.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will identify basic economic concepts.
description_plain: Your child can recognize simple ideas about money and how it is used.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain that different types of sources can be used to learn about the past.
description_plain: Your child knows that we can learn about the past from different kinds of sources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use words and phrases related to chronology and time to explain how things change including before/after and yesterday/today/tomorrow.
description_plain: Your child uses words like before, after, yesterday, today, and tomorrow to explain how things change over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how we honor people and events of the past.
description_plain: Your child understands how we remember and honor important people and events from the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how events of the past may have affected our community and the way we live today.
description_plain: Your child can explain how past events have influenced our community and the way we live now.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will understand that history relates to events and people of other times and places.
description_plain: Your child understands that history is about people and events from long ago and far away.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe family and community customs and traditions as basic elements of culture.
description_plain: Your child can describe family and community traditions and customs as part of culture.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe what makes one's community alike or different than other communities.
description_plain: Your child can explain what makes their community similar to or different from other communities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain that the school is part of a larger community and one's community is within the state of Oklahoma.
description_plain: Your child knows that their school is part of a bigger community and that this community is inside the state of Oklahoma.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the shape of the state of Oklahoma on a map.
description_plain: Your child can find and recognize the shape of Oklahoma on a map.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify basic cardinal directions and relative location terms.
description_plain: Your child can use basic directions like north, south, east, and west and words that describe location.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain that a globe is a model of the Earth and that a map is a drawing of a place; construct basic maps.
description_plain: Your child understands that a globe is a model of Earth and a map is a drawing of a place, and can make simple maps.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will demonstrate knowledge of basic physical and human geographic concepts.
description_plain: Your child knows basic ideas about places and people on Earth.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify other important United States symbols including the Statue of Liberty located in New York Harbor.
description_plain: Your child can name important U.S. symbols like the Statue of Liberty.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the purpose of the Pledge of Allegiance and explain appropriate flag etiquette.
description_plain: Your child knows why we say the Pledge of Allegiance and how to show respect for the flag.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the United States Flag as a symbol of the country, explaining the stripes as symbols for the first states and the stars as symbols for the current states in our country.
description_plain: Your child can identify the U.S. flag and explain that the stripes stand for the first states and the stars stand for all the states now.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify ways to be an active member of the community.
description_plain: Your child knows ways to be an active and helpful member of their community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the importance of rules, personal responsibilities, and natural consequences as a member of a family, class, and school.
description_plain: Your child understands why rules, responsibilities, and natural consequences are important at home, school, and in class.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will exhibit traits of good citizenship.
description_plain: Your child shows good citizenship by being responsible and respectful.

subject: Social Studies
description: engage in authentic inquiry to acquire, refine, and share knowledge through written presentations related to social studies.
description_plain: Your child can research social studies topics and share what they learn through writing.

subject: Social Studies
description: summarize and paraphrase, integrate evidence, and cite sources to create written products, research projects, and presentations for multiple purposes related to social studies content.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information, use evidence, and give credit to sources when writing reports and presentations about social studies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Students will apply effective communication skills by developing a variety of evidence-based written products designed for multiple purposes and tasks, in order to demonstrate their understandings of social studies concepts, ideas, and content.
description_plain: Your child uses good communication skills to write different kinds of papers that show what they understand about social studies.

subject: Social Studies
description: apply critical reading and thinking skills to interpret, evaluate, and respond to a variety of complex texts from historical, ethnic, and global perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can read and think carefully about complex social studies texts from different cultures and viewpoints.

subject: Social Studies
description: comprehend, evaluate, and synthesize textual sources to acquire and refine knowledge in the social studies.
description_plain: Your child can understand, judge, and combine information from social studies texts to learn more.

subject: Social Studies
description: Students will engage in critical, active reading of grade-level appropriate primary and secondary sources related to key social studies concepts, including frequent analysis and interpretation of informational sources.
description_plain: Your child reads and analyzes social studies materials to understand important ideas and information.

subject: Social Studies
description: analyze the principles of economic systems and develop an understanding of the benefits of a market system in local, national, and global settings.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different economic systems work and why market systems are helpful locally and worldwide.

subject: Social Studies
description: demonstrate an understanding of geographic concepts and develop mastery of geographic tools and ways of thinking in order to become geographically informed.
description_plain: Your child understands geography and uses tools like maps to learn about places.

subject: Social Studies
description: develop skills and practices which demonstrate an understanding that historical inquiry is based on the analysis and evaluation of evidence and its credibility.
description_plain: Your child learns how historians study and check evidence to understand the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: Students will utilize interdisciplinary tools and master the basic concepts of the social studies in order to acquire and apply content understanding in all related fields of study.
description_plain: Your child uses skills from different subjects to understand social studies topics better.

subject: Social Studies
description: demonstrate the ability to investigate problems taking into consideration multiple points of view represented in arguments, structure of an explanation, and other sources.
description_plain: Your child can look at problems from different viewpoints and understand how arguments are made.

subject: Social Studies
description: demonstrate the capability for developing essential, compelling, and supporting questions that address authentic civic issues.
description_plain: Your child can create important questions about real civic issues.

subject: Social Studies
description: Students will determine the kinds of sources that will be helpful in answering essential, compelling, and supporting questions addressing authentic civic issues.
description_plain: Your child knows how to find the best sources to answer important questions about civic topics.

subject: Social Studies
description: demonstrate understanding of the processes and rules by which groups of people make decisions, govern themselves, and address public problems.
description_plain: Your child understands how groups make decisions, govern themselves, and solve public problems.

subject: Social Studies
description: demonstrate an understanding of the important institutions of their society and the principles that these institutions are intended to reflect.
description_plain: Your child knows about important institutions in society and the ideas they represent.

subject: Social Studies
description: demonstrate an understanding of the virtues that citizens should use when interacting with each other and the virtues that guide official government institutions.
description_plain: Your child understands the good qualities citizens and government officials should have when working together.

subject: Social Studies
description: Students will understand the principles of government, the benefits of democratic systems, and their responsibilities as citizens.
description_plain: Your child understands how government works, why democracy is good, and what it means to be a responsible citizen.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the role of banks in the community.
description_plain: Your child can explain what banks do and why they are important in the community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and explain the roles of consumers and producers in the American economy.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between consumers who buy things and producers who make things in the economy.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe ways people are paid for their labor and how goods and services are purchased using money and credit.
description_plain: Your child understands how people earn money for work and how money and credit are used to buy goods and services.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the costs and benefits of spending and saving in order to meet needs and wants.
description_plain: Your child can explain why people spend or save money to get what they need or want.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will describe the characteristics of the American economic system.
description_plain: Your child can describe what makes the American economy special.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify primary sources and how they help us to learn about the past.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain original sources that help us learn about history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Read and construct basic timelines to understand the chronology of events in history.
description_plain: Your child can read and make simple timelines to show when events happened.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the contributions of people and groups who have shaped our history and ways we commemorate important places and events of the past.
description_plain: Your child can tell about important people and groups from history and how we remember special places and events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain why people may see events from different points of view.
description_plain: Your child understands that people may see the same event in different ways.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will examine important events and historical figures in the nation's past.
description_plain: Your child learns about important events and people in our nation's history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe community customs and traditions as basic elements of culture.
description_plain: Your child can describe customs and traditions that are part of a community's culture.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare the features of urban and rural communities.
description_plain: Your child can compare what makes cities and countryside areas different.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the difference between continents and oceans.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between continents and oceans.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify cardinal directions and use them to identify specific locations on a map.
description_plain: Your child can use directions like north, south, east, and west to find places on a map.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the difference between physical and political maps; construct basic maps of specific places.
description_plain: Your child can explain the difference between maps that show land features and those that show political borders, and can draw simple maps.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will demonstrate knowledge of basic geographic concepts.
description_plain: Your child learns basic ideas about geography.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify important symbols of the United States including the Bald Eagle and the Liberty Bell, and explain their meanings.
description_plain: Your child can name important U.S. symbols like the Bald Eagle and Liberty Bell and explain what they mean.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain patriotic traditions including The Pledge of Allegiance, describe appropriate flag etiquette and proper behavior during the playing of The Star-Spangled Banner.
description_plain: Your child understands patriotic traditions like The Pledge of Allegiance and knows how to behave respectfully during the national anthem and with the flag.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how citizens within communities work together to accomplish common tasks and fulfill roles of authority.
description_plain: Your child can describe how people in a community work together and follow leaders to get things done.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the need for written laws and the main purpose of government, including the concept of consequences for one's actions when a law or rule is violated.
description_plain: Your child understands why we need laws and government and knows that breaking rules has consequences.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will analyze their role as a citizen in a community.
description_plain: Your child learns about their role as a member of a community.

description: engage in authentic inquiry to acquire, refine, and share knowledge through written presentations related to social studies.
description_plain: Your child can research social studies topics and share what they learn through writing.

description_plain: Your child can summarize information, use evidence, and give credit to sources when writing reports and presentations about social studies.

description_plain: Your child understands how government works, why democracy is good, and what it means to be a responsible citizen.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how setting goals and creating a budget helps people pay for things they need and want.
description_plain: Your child can explain how setting goals and making a budget helps people pay for what they need and want.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the connection between taxes and community services, including schools, sanitation and water, fire and police protection, parks and recreation, libraries, and roads.
description_plain: Your child understands that taxes help pay for community services like schools, police, parks, and roads.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how barter and trade can lead to interdependence among communities.
description_plain: Your child can explain how trading goods helps communities depend on each other.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the importance of supply and demand in the consumer and producer relationship.
description_plain: Your child understands how supply and demand affect buyers and sellers.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will understand basic economic concepts in the American economy.
description_plain: Your child learns basic ideas about how the American economy works.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain possible reasons for events in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain why things happened in the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare different accounts of the same historical event using primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can compare different stories about the same event using original and secondhand sources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare perspectives of people in the past to people in the present.
description_plain: Your child can compare how people in the past and people today see things differently.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the contributions of people and groups who have shaped our history and who are honored by holidays and commemorative months.
description_plain: Your child learns about important people and groups who helped expand rights and are honored by holidays.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will examine the lives of notable Americans who expanded peoples' rights and freedoms through our history.
description_plain: Your child studies notable Americans who helped increase freedoms throughout history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe customs, traditions, clothing, food, housing, and music as basic elements of various cultures represented within the local community.
description_plain: Your child can describe different cultures in the community by their customs, clothes, food, homes, and music.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how communities modify the environment to meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child understands how communities change their environment to meet their needs.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and locate basic landforms, bodies of water, continents, and oceans on a map.
description_plain: Your child can find and name landforms, bodies of water, continents, and oceans on a map.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use political maps to locate the state of Oklahoma and the six bordering states.
description_plain: Your child can use maps to find Oklahoma and the six states that touch it.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe absolute and relative location using latitude, longitude, and hemispheres on basic maps and globes.
description_plain: Your child can describe exact and relative locations using latitude, longitude, and hemispheres on maps and globes.

subject: Social Studies
description: Construct basic maps using cardinal directions and map symbols.
description_plain: Your child can draw simple maps using directions and map symbols.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will describe the physical and human characteristics of their environment.
description_plain: Your child learns about the physical features and people in their environment.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how all people can play an important role in their community.
description_plain: Your child understands that everyone can play an important part in their community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the basic roles of national leaders including the President of the United States , the members of the United States Congress, and the justices of the Supreme Court.
description_plain: Your child can name the main jobs of national leaders like the President, members of Congress, and Supreme Court justices.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how active citizens participate in the government by voting to elect officials that represent them.
description_plain: Your child understands that people take part in government by voting for leaders who represent them.

subject: Social Studies
description: Summarize the five key individual rights and liberties protected by the First Amendment to the Constitution of the United States.
description_plain: Your child can explain the five important rights protected by the First Amendment, like freedom of speech and religion.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the Constitution of the United States as the structure for our national government.
description_plain: Your child understands that the Constitution is the plan that sets up how our national government works.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will explain the importance of the basic principles that provide the foundation of the American system of government.
description_plain: Your child will explain why the basic ideas behind the American government are important.
