subject: Language Arts
description: Students will expand and apply knowledge of grammar, usage, mechanics, and style to comprehend texts and communicate effectively.
description_plain: Your child can use grammar, punctuation, and writing style to understand what they read and to communicate clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language in speaking and writing according to purpose with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can use speaking and writing to share ideas for different purposes with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-level vocabulary to communicate ideas through speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child can use grade-level words to express ideas when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire new grade-level vocabulary, relate new words to prior knowledge, and apply vocabulary in new situations.
description_plain: Your child can learn new words, connect them to what they already know, and use them in new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to use grade-level resource materials (e.g., simple dictionary, glossary) to clarify the meaning of words.
description_plain: Your child can use simple dictionaries or glossaries to understand new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use word parts (e.g., affixes, Anglo-Saxon roots, stems) to define unfamiliar words with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of words like prefixes and roots to figure out new words with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context clues to determine the meaning of words with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to understand the meaning of words with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to determine relationships among words, including synonyms and antonyms.
description_plain: Your child can start to understand how words are related, like words that mean the same or opposite.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will expand and apply their spoken and reading vocabularies to speak, read, and write effectively.
description_plain: Your child can grow and use their speaking and reading vocabulary to talk, read, and write well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write an opinion about a topic and provide a reason to support the opinion with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can write an opinion about a topic and give a reason for it with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to write facts about a topic in response to a text read aloud to demonstrate understanding with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can write facts about a topic after listening to a story or text with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives incorporating characters, plot (i.e., beginning, middle, end), and a basic setting (i.e., time, place) with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can write stories with characters, a beginning, middle, end, and a simple setting with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to locate facts that are clearly stated in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find facts that are clearly stated in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to use details from a text to draw conclusions and make predictions.
description_plain: Your child can use details from a story to make guesses and conclusions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer basic questions (e.g., who, what, where, why, and when) about texts.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer simple questions like who, what, where, why, and when about stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Find textual evidence when provided with examples of literary elements:- setting ( i.e., time and place)- main characters and their traits
description_plain: Your child can find examples of story parts like setting and main characters when given hints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe who is telling a story with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author's purpose (i.e., tell a story, provide information) with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can understand why the author wrote the story, like to tell a story or give information, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will apply critical thinking skills to reading and writing.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully about what they read and write to understand and explain ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will routinely use a recursive process to publish final drafts for an authentic audience (e.g., reading aloud, author's chair).
description_plain: Your child can share their finished writing by reading it aloud or sharing with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will correctly spell grade-level, highly decodable words (e.g., cup, like, cart) and common, irregularly spelled sight words (e.g., the) while editing using resources as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-level words and common sight words correctly when editing, using help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will revise drafts by adding and/or deleting text.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by adding or removing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will develop drafts by sequencing the action in a story or details about a topic through writing sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write sentences that put story events or facts in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will develop and edit drafts using appropriate spacing between letters, words, and sentences using left-to-right and top-to-bottom progression.
description_plain: Your child can write and edit drafts with proper spacing and writing direction.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will retell facts and details from an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can retell important facts and details from an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will sequence the plot (i.e., beginning, middle, and end) of a story.
description_plain: Your child can put the parts of a story in order: beginning, middle, and end.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will discriminate between fiction and nonfiction genres.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between fiction and nonfiction books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will identify the topic or main idea with some supporting details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea and some details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will use a variety of recursive reading and writing processes.
description_plain: Your child uses different steps over and over to read and write better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly spell high-frequency and irregularly-spelled words by using common letter/sound correspondences and recalling the irregular parts of words (e.g., the: th- is a common letter/sound correspondence, -e is irregular).
description_plain: Your child can spell common and tricky words by using letter sounds and remembering parts that don’t follow the rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct spelling when writing the following sounds in words:- consonants- short vowels- digraphs- consonant blends- vowel-consonant-silent e
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly by using sounds for consonants, short vowels, blends, and silent e.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally and accurately read grade-level text at a smooth rate with expression that connotes comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read first-grade level books out loud smoothly and with feeling to show they understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand their sight word vocabulary by reading regularly- and irregularly-spelled words in isolation and context with increasing automaticity.
description_plain: Your child will learn to quickly recognize common and tricky words when reading alone or in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words by applying knowledge of structural analysis:- compound words- inflectional endings (e.g., -s, -ed, -ing)
description_plain: Your child can figure out words by breaking them into parts like compound words and endings like -s, -ed, and -ing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words by applying knowledge of syllable types in one-syllable words:- closed- open
description_plain: Your child can sound out one-syllable words by knowing if the syllable is open or closed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode one-syllable phonetically-regular words by using their knowledge of the following phonics skills:- single consonants (e.g., b = /b/, f = /f/, t = /t/)- short vowel sounds (i.e., ă, ĕ, ĭ, ŏ, ŭ)- consonant blends (e.g., bl, br, cr)- consonant digraphs and trigraphs (e.g., sh, tch)- vowel-consonant-silent e (e.g., lake)- r-controlled vowels (i.e., ar, er, ir, or, ur)- vowel digraphs (e.g., ea, oa, ee)
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words by using sounds of letters, blends, digraphs, silent e, r-controlled vowels, and vowel pairs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly form letters and use appropriate spacing for letters, words, and sentences using left-to-right and top-to-bottom progression.
description_plain: Your child can write letters neatly with correct spacing between letters, words, and sentences from left to right and top to bottom.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., capitalization of the first word, ending punctuation: period, exclamation point, question mark).
description_plain: Your child understands that sentences start with a capital letter and end with a period, question mark, or exclamation point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add, delete, and substitute phonemes in one-syllable spoken words with 3-5 phonemes (e.g., add /c/ to the beginning of at to say cat; delete the /p/ from pin, to say in; substitute the /o/ in stop with /e/ to say step).
description_plain: Your child can change sounds in short spoken words by adding, removing, or swapping sounds to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment phonemes in spoken words with 4-6 phonemes into individual phonemes (e.g., string = /s/ /t/ /r/ /i/ /ng/).
description_plain: Your child can break apart spoken words with 4 to 6 sounds into each individual sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend phonemes to form spoken words with 4-6 phonemes, including consonant blends (e.g., /s/ /t/ /r/ /i/ /ng/ = string).
description_plain: Your child can put together individual sounds, including blends, to say whole spoken words with 4 to 6 sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce medial sounds in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and say the middle sound in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onset and rime in spoken words (e.g., /ch/+ at = chat).
description_plain: Your child can blend and separate the beginning sound and the rest of the word in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, segment, blend, and delete syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count, break apart, blend, and remove syllables in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will develop foundational skills for reading and writing proficiency by working with sounds, letters, and text.
description_plain: Your child builds important reading and writing skills by working with sounds, letters, and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details expressing their ideas.
description_plain: Your child can talk about people, places, things, and events with details to share their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer relevant questions to seek help or get information to confirm understanding.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to get help or check they understand something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about various topics and texts, including their own writing, with peers in small and large groups.
description_plain: Your child can join group talks about different topics and their own writing with classmates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work respectfully with peers.
description_plain: Work respectfully with peers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow simple two- and three-step oral directions.
description_plain: Your child can follow simple spoken directions that have two or three steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively listen using agreed-upon discussion rules.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully and follows the rules during group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will listen and speak effectively in a variety of situations.
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and speak clearly in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write independently using print and/or typing over various lengths of time for a variety of purposes.
description_plain: Your child can write on their own, by hand or typing, for different reasons and lengths of time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select texts for academic and personal purposes and read independently for extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can pick books for school or fun and read by themselves for a long time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will read and write independently for a variety of purposes and periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can read and write on their own for different reasons and for different amounts of time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of writing, sound, visual content, and/or movement to communicate ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas and feelings using writing, sounds, pictures, or movement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explore and compare ideas and topics in multimodal content.
description_plain: Your child can explore and compare ideas in different types of media like videos, pictures, and text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will comprehend and communicate knowledge through alphabetic, aural, visual, spatial, and/or gestural content.
description_plain: Your child can understand and share ideas using letters, sounds, pictures, space, and gestures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and share relevant information for various purposes.
description_plain: Your child can organize and share important information for different reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information found during group or individual research, using graphic organizers or other aids.
description_plain: Your child can organize information they find during research using charts or other tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generate a list of topics and questions about an area of interest for research.
description_plain: Your child can make a list of topics and questions they want to learn about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult various text reference sources to gather information (i.e., title page, table of contents, glossary, index).
description_plain: Your child can use parts of books like the table of contents, glossary, and index to find information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and use graphic and text features to understand texts:- photos- illustrations- titles- labels- headings- charts- graphs- captions- subheadings
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, titles, labels, charts, and other features to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create their own questions to find information on their topic.
description_plain: Your child can create their own questions to help find information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will engage in inquiry to acquire, refine, and communicate accurate information.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, find accurate information, and share what they learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a colon to indicate time (e.g., The bell rings at 3:15.).
description_plain: Your child can use a colon correctly to show time, like 3:15.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates (e.g., September 6, 2020).
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly in dates, like September 6, 2020.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use apostrophes to form simple contractions (e.g., isn't, aren't, can't).
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes to write contractions like isn't, aren't, and can't.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use periods with declarative and imperative sentences, question marks with interrogative sentences, and exclamation points with exclamatory sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use periods, question marks, and exclamation points correctly at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Punctuate initials and capitalize holidays, product names, initials, and months and days of the week.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters for holidays, product names, initials, months, and days, and punctuate initials properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use nouns, verbs, and adjectives to add clarity and variety to their writing.
description_plain: Your child can use nouns, verbs, and adjectives to make their writing clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences that begin with a capital letter and conclude with an end mark.
description_plain: Your child can write simple and compound sentences that start with a capital letter and end with the right punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize parts of speech in sentences:- common, proper, and irregular plural nouns- tenses of verbs (i.e., past, present, future)- the simple subject and simple predicate of a sentence- descriptive adjectives and articles (i.e., a, an, the) as adjectives- prepositions- singular and plural personal pronouns and the nouns they replace- the conjunctions and, or, and but- -ly adverbs
description_plain: Your child can recognize parts of speech like nouns, verbs, adjectives, prepositions, pronouns, conjunctions, and adverbs in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize simple and compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between simple and compound sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will expand and apply knowledge of grammar, usage, mechanics, and style to comprehend texts and communicate effectively.
description_plain: Your child can use grammar, punctuation, and writing style to understand what they read and to communicate clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language in speaking and writing according to purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can use the right words and style when speaking or writing for different purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-level vocabulary to communicate ideas through speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child can use grade-appropriate words to share ideas when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire new grade-level vocabulary, relate new words to prior knowledge, and apply vocabulary in new situations.
description_plain: Your child can learn new words, connect them to what they already know, and use them in new situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade-level resource materials (e.g., simple dictionary, glossary) to clarify the meaning of words.
description_plain: Your child can use simple dictionaries or glossaries to understand the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use word parts (e.g., affixes, Anglo-Saxon roots, stems) to define and determine the meaning of new words.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words by looking at parts like prefixes and roots.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context clues to determine the meaning of words.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentences around a word to understand what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine relationships among words, including synonyms, antonyms, and simple multiple-meaning words.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related, like words that mean the same, opposite, or have more than one meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will expand and apply their spoken and reading vocabularies to speak, read, and write effectively.
description_plain: Your child can grow and use their speaking and reading vocabulary to talk, read, and write well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write an opinion about a topic and provide reasons as support in a paragraph.
description_plain: Your child can write a paragraph stating their opinion on a topic and give reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write facts about a topic and include a main idea with supporting details in a paragraph.
description_plain: Your child can write a paragraph that shares facts about a topic with a main idea and supporting details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives incorporating characters, plot (i.e., beginning, middle, end), and a basic setting (i.e., time, place).
description_plain: Your child can write a story with characters, a beginning, middle, and end, and describe when and where it happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the structure of an informational text with prompting:- description- sequential
description_plain: Your child can describe how an informational text is organized, like by describing things or telling steps in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate facts that are clearly stated in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find facts that are clearly stated in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use details from the literary or informational text to draw conclusions and make predictions.
description_plain: Your child can use details from stories or information texts to make guesses about what might happen next or what the author means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Find examples of literary devices:- simile- alliteration- onomatopoeia
description_plain: Your child can find examples of fun writing tools like similes, alliteration, and sound words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Find examples of literary elements:- setting (i.e., time and place)- plot (i.e., beginning, middle, end)- characters and their traits
description_plain: Your child can find parts of a story like where and when it happens, what happens first, next, and last, and who the characters are.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine whether a grade-level literary text is narrated in first- or third-person point of view with prompting.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a story is told by a character inside the story or by someone outside the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the author's purpose (i.e., tell a story, provide information).
description_plain: Your child understands why the author wrote the story or text, like to tell a story or give information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will apply critical thinking skills to reading and writing.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully about what they read and write to understand and explain ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely use a recursive process to publish final drafts for an authentic audience (e.g., reading aloud, author's chair).
description_plain: Your child can revise and share their finished writing with others, like reading it out loud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly spell grade-level words while editing using resources as needed.
description_plain: Your child can check and fix spelling mistakes using tools like dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise drafts by adding, deleting, and/or moving text.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by adding, removing, or moving words and sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Edit drafts using appropriate spacing between letters, words, and sentences.
description_plain: Your child can edit their writing to make sure letters, words, and sentences have the right spaces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prewrite and develop drafts by sequencing the action in a story or details about a topic through writing sentences.
description_plain: Your child can plan and write stories or information by putting events or facts in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to summarize facts and details from an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can start to tell the main facts and details from an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to summarize the plot of a story to include the beginning, middle, and end.
description_plain: Your child can start to tell the main events of a story, including the beginning, middle, and end.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify elements of various genres in fiction and nonfiction texts.
description_plain: Your child can recognize different types of stories and information books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main idea and supporting details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea and important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will use a variety of recursive reading and writing processes.
description_plain: Your child uses different steps over and over to read and write better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use structural analysis to correctly spell the following parts of words:- common prefixes- common suffixes- common spelling rules related to adding prefixes and suffixes (e.g., dropping the final -e, doubling a consonant)
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly by understanding common beginnings and endings and spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct spelling when writing the following syllable types in single-syllable and multisyllabic words:- closed- open- vowel-consonant-silent e- r-controlled
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly using different syllable types like closed, open, and silent e.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct spelling when writing the following sounds in words:- digraphs- trigraphs- vowel digraphs- r-controlled
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly by knowing sounds like digraphs, trigraphs, and r-controlled vowels.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally and accurately read grade-level text at a smooth rate with expression that connotes comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level text out loud smoothly and with expression that shows they understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand their sight word vocabulary by reading regularly- and irregularly-spelled words in isolation and context with increasing automaticity.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read many common words quickly and easily.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words by applying knowledge of structural analysis:- compound words- inflectional endings (e.g., -s, -ed, -ing)- contractions- abbreviations- common roots and related prefixes and suffixes
description_plain: Your child can read words by breaking them into parts like compound words, endings, contractions, and common roots.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words by applying knowledge of all major syllable types:- closed- open- vowel digraphs- vowel-consonant-silent e- r-controlled- consonant +le
description_plain: Your child can read words by knowing different syllable types like closed, open, vowel digraphs, and consonant + le.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode one- and two-syllable words by using their knowledge of the following phonics skills:- single consonants, including those with two different sounds (e.g., soft and hard c [cent, cat] and g [gem, goat])- consonant blends (e.g., bl, br, cr, spr, spl)- consonant digraphs and trigraphs (e.g., sh, tch)- vowel-consonant-silent e (e.g., lake)- r-controlled vowels (i.e., ar, er, ir or, ur)- vowel digraphs (e.g., ea, oa, ee)- vowel diphthongs (i.e., vowel combinations having two vowel sounds [e.g., oi as in boil, oy as in boy])- schwa (e.g., banana)- silent letter combinations (knew, could, ghost)
description_plain: Your child can read one- and two-syllable words by using sounds of letters, blends, digraphs, silent e, r-controlled vowels, vowel pairs, and silent letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly form letters in print and use appropriate spacing for letters, words, and sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write letters neatly and leave the right spaces between letters, words, and sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add, delete, and substitute phonemes in spoken words with 5-6 phonemes (e.g., add /th/ to the beginning of rowing to say throwing; delete the /r/ in trips to say tips; substitute the /l/ in clank with /r/ to say crank).
description_plain: Your child can change sounds in spoken words by adding, taking away, or swapping sounds to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will develop foundational skills for reading and writing proficiency by working with sounds, letters, and text.
description_plain: Your child builds important reading and writing skills by working with sounds, letters, and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with relevant facts descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can talk about a topic, tell a story, or share an experience clearly with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer relevant questions to seek help, get information, or clarify information to confirm understanding.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to get help or understand information better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about various topics and texts, including their own writing, with peers in small and large groups.
description_plain: Your child can join group talks about different topics and their own writing with classmates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work respectfully in groups by sharing responsibility for collaborative work and recognizing individual contributions made by each group member.
description_plain: Your child can work well in groups by sharing tasks and appreciating what others do.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow multi-step oral directions.
description_plain: Your child can follow spoken directions that have several steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively listen using agreed-upon discussion rules.
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and follow agreed rules during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will listen and speak effectively in a variety of situations.
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and speak clearly in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write independently using print, cursive, and/or typing for various lengths of time, choosing modes and genres to suit their audience and purpose.
description_plain: Your child can write on their own using print, cursive, or typing for different amounts of time and choose the best way to write for their readers and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read selected texts independently and for various lengths of time, choosing genres to suit and expand their personal preferences and purposes.
description_plain: Your child can read different types of books on their own for various amounts of time, picking stories that they like and that help them learn new things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will read and write independently for a variety of purposes and periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can read and write on their own for different reasons and for different amounts of time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate their ideas, thoughts, and feelings by combining two or more kinds of content:- writing/alphabetic- sound, visual, and/or spatial- movement
description_plain: Your child can share their ideas and feelings by combining writing with sounds, pictures, or movements.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate and use information from a variety of alphabetic, aural, visual, spatial, and/or gestural content to compare perspectives about ideas and topics.
description_plain: Your child can find and use information from different sources like books, sounds, pictures, or gestures to compare different opinions about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will comprehend and communicate knowledge through alphabetic, aural, visual, spatial, and/or gestural content.
description_plain: Your child can understand and share ideas using letters, sounds, pictures, space, and gestures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative texts independently for short timeframes (e.g., a single sitting or a day or two) that organize related information about a topic and convey details from a single source.
description_plain: Your child can write short reports on a topic by organizing facts from one source, finishing the work in one or two days.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to organize information found during research, following a modified citation style (i.e., author, title, publication year).
description_plain: Your child can start organizing information they find during research and list the author, title, and year of the source.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose a topic of interest and generate several questions about it for research.
description_plain: Your child can pick a topic they are interested in and come up with several questions to learn more about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to determine the relevance of the information gathered.
description_plain: Your child can begin to decide which information they find is important and useful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and use text features (e.g., graphics, captions, subheadings, italics, charts, tables, legends) to comprehend informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a text like pictures, captions, headings, and charts to better understand informational books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct research to answer questions, including self-generated questions, and to build knowledge.
description_plain: Your child can do research to answer questions they ask themselves and to learn new facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will engage in inquiry to acquire, refine, and communicate accurate information.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, find accurate information, and share what they learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use quotation marks to indicate dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can use quotation marks correctly to show when someone is speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a colon to indicate time.
description_plain: Use a colon to indicate time.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child knows how to use tools like dictionaries and thesauruses to find meanings, pronunciations, and other details about words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use word parts (e.g., affixes, Anglo-Saxon, Greek, and Latin roots, stems) to define and determine the meaning of increasingly complex words.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meanings of harder words by looking at parts like prefixes, roots, and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context clues, connotation, and denotation to determine or clarify the meaning of words or distinguish among multiple-meaning words.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from sentences and word meanings to understand or choose the right meaning of words with multiple meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationships among synonyms, antonyms, and analogies.
description_plain: Your child understands how words that mean the same, opposite, or relate to each other help make meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will expand and apply their spoken and reading vocabularies to speak, read, and write effectively.
description_plain: Your child can grow and use their speaking and reading vocabulary to talk, read, and write well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend narrative, informative, and argumentative writing to suit their audience and purpose.
description_plain: Your child can mix storytelling, facts, and opinions in their writing to fit who they are writing for and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose argumentative essays, reviews, or op-eds that:- introduce precise, informed claims- include a defensible thesis- acknowledge counterclaims or alternate perspectives- organize claims, counterclaims, and evidence in a logical sequence- provide the most relevant evidence to develop balanced arguments, using credible sources- use sentence variety and word choice to create clarity and concision- use style and tone that suits the audience and purpose
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces that clearly state their claim, include reasons and evidence, consider other views, and use clear and interesting language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose informative essays, reports, or technical writing that:- objectively introduce and develop topics- include a defensible thesis- incorporate evidence (e.g., specific facts, details, charts and graphs, data)- maintain an organized structure- use sentence variety and word choice to create clarity and concision- establish and maintain a formal style- emulate literary devices from mentor texts
description_plain: Your child writes reports or essays that explain topics clearly with facts and details, organized in a way that makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose narratives reflecting real or imagined experiences that:- include engaging plots involving well-developed, complex characters resolving conflicts- establish narrator(s) that enhance(s) the narrative- are intentionally sequenced in a way to achieve a specific effect (e.g., create suspense, establish mood, reflect theme)- provide clear descriptions, using precise language, sensory details, and dialogue- include varied syntax to enhance readability- emulate literary elements and/or literary devices from mentor texts
description_plain: Your child writes stories with interesting plots and characters, using clear descriptions, dialogue, and varied sentence styles to create mood or suspense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics, using textual evidence to support their claims and inferences.
description_plain: Your child compares two or more texts about similar topics and uses examples from the texts to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how informational text structures support the author's purpose.
description_plain: Your child understands how the way an informational text is organized helps the author share their main message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the validity of a speaker's argument:- distinguish the kinds of evidence (e.g., logical, empirical, anecdotal)- distinguish substantiated from unsubstantiated claims- analyze rhetorical appeals (i.e., ethos, logos, pathos)- identify bias- identify logical fallacies
description_plain: Your child can judge if a speaker’s argument is strong by looking at the types of evidence, spotting bias or mistakes, and understanding how the speaker tries to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how literary devices impact theme, mood, and/or tone, using textual evidence:- figurative language (i.e., simile, metaphor, personification, hyperbole, imagery, symbolism)- sound devices (i.e., onomatopoeia, alliteration, assonance)- irony (i.e., verbal, situational, dramatic)
description_plain: Your child explains how things like similes, metaphors, sound effects, and irony affect the feeling or message of a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how literary elements impact theme, mood, and/or tone, using textual evidence:- setting- plot structure (e.g., foreshadowing, flashback, in media res)- conflict (i.e., internal, external)- characters (e.g., protagonist, antagonist)- characterization (i.e., direct, indirect)- point of view (e.g., narrator reliability)- archetypes
description_plain: Your child looks at parts of a story like setting, plot, characters, and point of view to understand its mood and message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate authors' perspectives and explain how those perspectives contribute to the meanings of texts.
description_plain: Your child thinks about the author’s point of view and explains how it helps shape the meaning of the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the extent to which historical, cultural, and/or global perspectives affect authors' stylistic choices in grade-level literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how an author’s background and culture influence their writing style and choices.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will apply critical thinking skills to reading and writing.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully about what they read and write to understand and explain ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively publish final drafts for an authentic audience (e.g., publishing digitally, community and professional audiences, newspapers and magazines, entering contests).
description_plain: Your child regularly shares their finished writing with real audiences, like online readers, community groups, or contests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively use resources to edit for grammar, usage, mechanics, and format in order to finalize drafts for publication.
description_plain: Your child uses tools and resources to check and fix grammar, spelling, punctuation, and formatting before sharing their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively revise drafts for organization, transitions, sentence variety, and consistent tone and point of view in order to improve coherence and meaning.
description_plain: Your child revises their writing to improve organization, smooth transitions, sentence variety, and consistent tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively develop drafts, applying organizational structure(s) (e.g., description, compare/contrast, sequential, problem/solution, cause/effect, etc.).
description_plain: Your child develops drafts by organizing ideas clearly using different structures like compare/contrast or cause/effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively prewrite (e.g., develop and organize ideas).
description_plain: Your child regularly plans and organizes their ideas before writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify characteristics of genres and analyze how they enhance comprehension of fiction, poetry, drama, and nonfiction.
description_plain: Your child recognizes different types of stories and texts and understands how their features help with comprehension.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the main ideas and paraphrase significant parts of increasingly complex texts.
description_plain: Your child can summarize main ideas and restate important parts of more difficult texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will use a variety of recursive reading and writing processes.
description_plain: Your child uses different steps over and over to read and write better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct formal and informal presentations in a variety of contexts supporting their message with evidence and using verbal and nonverbal cues.
description_plain: Your child gives presentations in different settings, supporting their points with evidence and using clear speaking and body language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules as they engage in collaborative discussions about what they are reading and writing, expressing their own ideas clearly, building on the ideas of others, and respectfully disagreeing when necessary in pairs, diverse groups, and whole-class settings.
description_plain: Your child follows discussion rules, shares their ideas clearly, listens to others, and respectfully disagrees when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work effectively and respectfully in diverse groups by showing willingness to make necessary compromises to accomplish a goal, sharing responsibility for collaborative work, and recognizing individual contributions
description_plain: Your child works well and respectfully with different people, shares responsibility, and appreciates everyone’s contributions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively listen in order to analyze and evaluate speakers' verbal and nonverbal messages by asking questions to clarify purpose and perspective.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully to understand and evaluate what speakers say, asking questions to clarify their purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively listen using agreed-upon discussion rules with control of verbal and nonverbal cues.
description_plain: Your child listens actively during discussions, following agreed rules and using appropriate speaking and body language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will listen and speak effectively in a variety of situations.
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and speak clearly in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write independently using print, cursive, and/or typing for various lengths of time, intentionally selecting and combining modes and genres to suit their audiences and purposes.
description_plain: Your child writes independently by hand or typing for different amounts of time, choosing styles and types of writing that fit their audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select texts for specific purposes and read independently for extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child picks texts for specific reasons and reads on their own for longer periods.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will read and write independently for a variety of purposes and periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can read and write on their own for different reasons and for different amounts of time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write independently using print, cursive, and/or typing for various lengths of time, intentionally selecting and combining modes and genres to suit their audiences and purposes.
description_plain: Your child writes independently by hand or typing for different amounts of time, choosing styles and types of writing that fit their audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will comprehend and communicate knowledge through alphabetic, aural, visual, spatial, and/or gestural content.
description_plain: Your child can understand and share ideas using letters, sounds, pictures, space, and gestures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present research in longer formats (e.g., sustained research projects, process essays) and shorter, informal formats (e.g., single-day research projects, informal discussion) to a variety of audiences.
description_plain: Your child presents research projects, both long and short, to different audiences clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quotes, paraphrases, and summaries from research, following a consistent citation style (e.g., MLA, APA) to avoid plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child includes quotes and information from research in their writing and cites sources properly to avoid plagiarism.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop a clear, concise, defensible thesis statement.
description_plain: Your child writes clear and focused thesis statements that can be supported with evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate and refine a viable research question.
description_plain: Your child creates and improves research questions that can be explored in depth.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the relevance, reliability, and validity of the information gathered.
description_plain: Your child judges if the information they find is relevant, trustworthy, and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Synthesize relevant information from a variety of primary and secondary sources, following ethical and legal citation guidelines.
description_plain: Your child combines information from many sources and cites them correctly following ethical rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Find and comprehend information about a topic, using their own viable research questions.
description_plain: Your child can find and understand information about a topic by asking their own good research questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will engage in inquiry to acquire, refine, and communicate accurate information.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, find accurate information, and share what they learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct semicolon mechanics.
description_plain: Your child can write sentences using semicolons correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct italics mechanics.
description_plain: Your child can use italics properly when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate omission from quoted material and brackets to indicate an addition or clarification to quoted material.
description_plain: Your child can use ellipses to show missing words in quotes and brackets to add or explain words in quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a colon to reveal information.
description_plain: Your child can use a colon to introduce or reveal information in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct comma mechanics.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct apostrophe mechanics.
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes correctly to show possession or contractions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct end mark mechanics.
description_plain: Your child can use correct punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct capitalization mechanics.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters correctly in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate their use of Standard American English (i.e., grammar, mechanics, and usage) in writing, presentations, and/or multimodal literacies to convey specific meanings and ideas, intentionally departing from standard usage to achieve a specific effect.
description_plain: Your child can use proper grammar and writing rules but also change them on purpose to create a special effect in writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add clarity, variety, and/or style to their writing and presentations with parts of speech and various types of phrases and clauses.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing and speaking clearer and more interesting by using different parts of speech and types of phrases and clauses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use or revise for active/passive voice and parallel structure in words, phrases, and/or clauses for effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize noun, verb, adjectival, and adverbial phrases and explain their effect in particular sentences.

description: Recognize and analyze the effect of parallel structure, active and passive voice, and various types of phrases and clauses to convey specific meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose argumentative essays, reviews, or op-eds that:- introduce precise, informed claims- include a defensible thesis- acknowledge counterclaims or alternate perspectives- organize claims, counterclaims, and evidence in a logical sequence- provide the most relevant evidence to develop balanced arguments, using credible sources- use sentence variety and word choice to create clarity and concision- use style and tone that suits the audience and purpose
description_plain: Your child writes opinion essays or reviews that clearly state their claims, include a strong main idea, consider other views, organize ideas logically, use good evidence, and match style to the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose informative essays, reports, or technical writing that:- objectively introduce and develop topics- include a defensible thesis- incorporate evidence (e.g., specific facts, details, charts and graphs, data)- maintain an organized structure- use sentence variety and word choice to create clarity and concision- establish and maintain a formal style- emulate literary devices from mentor texts
description_plain: Your child writes informative essays or reports that clearly introduce topics, include a strong main idea, use facts and data, stay organized, use clear language, and keep a formal tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose narratives reflecting real or imagined experiences that:- include engaging plots involving well-developed, complex characters resolving conflicts- establish narrator(s) that enhance(s) the narrative- are intentionally sequenced in a way to achieve a specific effect (e.g., create suspense, establish mood, reflect theme)- provide clear descriptions, using precise language, sensory details, and dialogue- include varied syntax to enhance readability- emulate literary elements and/or literary devices from mentor texts
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events with interesting plots, well-developed characters, clear descriptions, and varied sentence styles to create effects like suspense or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how two or more texts address similar themes or topics, using textual evidence to support their claims and inferences.
description_plain: Your child compares how two or more texts handle similar themes or topics using evidence from the texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how informational text structures support the author's purpose.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how the way informational texts are organized helps the author’s purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how authors writing on the same issue reached different conclusions because of differences in assumptions, evidence, reasoning, and viewpoints, including examining rhetorical appeals, bias, and use of logical fallacies.
description_plain: Your child evaluates why authors writing about the same topic may have different conclusions by looking at their assumptions, evidence, reasoning, bias, and use of persuasive techniques.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how literary devices impact theme, mood, and/or tone, using textual evidence:- figurative language (i.e., simile, metaphor, personification, hyperbole, imagery, symbolism)- sound devices (i.e., onomatopoeia, alliteration, assonance)- irony (i.e., verbal, situational, dramatic)
description_plain: Your child explains how literary devices like similes, metaphors, sound effects, and irony affect the theme, mood, or tone of a text using examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how literary elements impact theme, mood, and/or tone, using textual evidence:- setting- plot structure (e.g., foreshadowing, flashback, in media res)- conflict (i.e., internal, external)- characters (e.g., protagonist, antagonist)- characterization (i.e., direct, indirect)- point of view (e.g., narrator reliability)- archetypes
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story like setting, plot, conflict, characters, and point of view affect the main message and feelings in the text, using examples from what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate authors' perspectives and explain how those perspectives contribute to the meanings of texts.
description_plain: Your child can understand an author's point of view and explain how it helps shape the meaning of the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the extent to which historical, cultural, and/or global perspectives affect authors' stylistic choices in grade-level literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how history, culture, and world views influence the way authors write and choose their style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will apply critical thinking skills to reading and writing.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully about what they read and write to understand and explain ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively publish final drafts for an authentic audience (e.g., publishing digitally, community and professional audiences, newspapers and magazines, entering contests).
description_plain: Your child regularly shares their finished writing with real audiences, like online readers, community groups, or contests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively use resources to edit for grammar, usage, mechanics, and format in order to finalize drafts for publication.
description_plain: Your child uses tools and resources to check and fix grammar, spelling, punctuation, and formatting before sharing their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively revise drafts for organization, transitions, sentence variety, and consistent tone and point of view in order to improve coherence and meaning.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by reorganizing ideas, adding transitions, varying sentences, and keeping a steady tone and point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively develop drafts, applying organizational structure(s) (e.g., description, compare/contrast, sequential, problem/solution, cause/effect, etc.).
description_plain: Your child develops their writing drafts by organizing ideas using different structures like description, comparison, or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively prewrite (e.g., develop and organize ideas).
description_plain: Your child plans and organizes their ideas before they start writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify characteristics of genres and analyze how they enhance comprehension of fiction, poetry, drama, and nonfiction.
description_plain: Your child can recognize different types of writing like stories, poems, plays, and nonfiction, and explain how these types help them understand the text better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the main ideas and paraphrase significant parts of increasingly complex texts.
description_plain: Your child can summarize the main ideas and restate important parts of more challenging texts in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will use a variety of recursive reading and writing processes.
description_plain: Your child uses different steps over and over to read and write better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct formal and informal presentations in a variety of contexts supporting their message with evidence and using verbal and nonverbal cues.
description_plain: Your child can give formal and informal presentations, supporting their points with evidence and using body language and voice to help their message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules as they engage in collaborative discussions about what they are reading and writing, expressing their own ideas clearly, building on the ideas of others, and respectfully disagreeing when necessary in pairs, diverse groups, and whole-class settings.
description_plain: Your child follows discussion rules, shares their ideas clearly, listens to others, and respectfully disagrees when talking about reading and writing in groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work effectively and respectfully in diverse groups by showing willingness to make necessary compromises to accomplish a goal, sharing responsibility for collaborative work, and recognizing individual contributions.
description_plain: Your child works well and respectfully with different people, shares responsibility, and is willing to compromise to reach group goals.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively listen in order to analyze and evaluate speakers' verbal and nonverbal messages by asking questions to clarify purpose and perspective.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully to understand and evaluate what speakers say and do, asking questions to clarify their purpose and viewpoint.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively listen using agreed-upon discussion rules with control of verbal and nonverbal cues.
description_plain: Your child listens actively during discussions, following agreed rules and using appropriate body language and speaking habits.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will listen and speak effectively in a variety of situations.
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and speak clearly in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write independently using print, cursive, and/or typing for various lengths of time, intentionally selecting and combining modes and genres to suit their audiences and purposes.
description_plain: Your child writes independently by hand or typing for different amounts of time, choosing styles and types of writing that fit their audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select texts for specific purposes and read independently for extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child picks reading materials for specific reasons and reads on their own for longer periods.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will read and write independently for a variety of purposes and periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can read and write on their own for different reasons and for different amounts of time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging multimodal content that intentionally enhances understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence for diverse audiences.
description_plain: Your child creates engaging content using different media (like text, images, or videos) to clearly explain their ideas and evidence to different audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and evaluate the techniques used in a variety of multimodal content and how they contribute to meaning.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how different media techniques work together to create meaning in various types of content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will comprehend and communicate knowledge through alphabetic, aural, visual, spatial, and/or gestural content.
description_plain: Your child can understand and share ideas using letters, sounds, pictures, space, and gestures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present research in longer formats (e.g., sustained research projects, process essays) and shorter, informal formats (e.g., single-day research projects, informal discussion) to a variety of audiences.
description_plain: Your child presents research projects in both long and short formats to different audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quotes, paraphrases, and summaries from research, following a consistent citation style (e.g., MLA, APA) to avoid plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child includes quotes and information from research in their writing, giving proper credit to avoid plagiarism.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop a clear, concise, defensible thesis statement.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear and strong main statement (thesis) to support their research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate and refine a viable research question.
description_plain: Your child creates and improves a good research question to guide their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the relevance, reliability, and validity of the information gathered.
description_plain: Your child checks if the information they find is relevant, trustworthy, and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Synthesize relevant information from a variety of primary and secondary sources, following ethical and legal citation guidelines.
description_plain: Your child combines information from different sources and gives proper credit following ethical rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Find and comprehend information about a topic, using their own viable research questions.
description_plain: Your child finds and understands information about a topic using their own research questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will engage in inquiry to acquire, refine, and communicate accurate information.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, find accurate information, and share what they learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct semicolon mechanics.
description_plain: Your child writes using semicolons correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct italics mechanics.
description_plain: Your child writes using italics correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate omission from quoted material and brackets to indicate an addition or clarification to quoted material.
description_plain: Your child uses ellipses to show missing words and brackets to add or explain words in quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a colon or dash to reveal information in a sentence.
description_plain: Your child uses colons or dashes to introduce or emphasize information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct comma mechanics.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct apostrophe mechanics.
description_plain: Your child uses apostrophes correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct end mark mechanics.
description_plain: Your child uses correct punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write using correct capitalization mechanics.
description_plain: Your child uses capitalization correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of Standard American English (i.e., grammar, mechanics, and usage) in writing, presentations, and/or multimodal literacies to convey specific meanings and ideas, intentionally departing from standard usage to achieve a specific effect.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and writing rules in their writing and presentations, and can break rules on purpose to create special effects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add clarity, variety, and/or style to their writing and presentations with parts of speech and various types of phrases and clauses, intentionally breaking the rules for effect when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing and speaking by using different parts of speech and sentence parts, sometimes breaking rules for effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize noun, verb, adjectival, and adverbial phrases and explain their effect in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child recognizes different types of phrases (noun, verb, adjective, adverb) and explains how they affect sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply their knowledge of syntax (e.g., parallel structure, active and passive voice, and phrases/clauses) to analyze and evaluate a variety of texts, understanding that usage and convention change over time.
description_plain: Your child uses their knowledge of sentence structure and grammar to understand and evaluate texts, knowing that language rules can change over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will expand and apply knowledge of grammar, usage, mechanics, and style to comprehend texts and communicate effectively.
description_plain: Your child can use grammar, punctuation, and writing style to understand what they read and to communicate clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select language to create a specific effect in writing according to purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child chooses words carefully to create a specific effect based on who they are writing for and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise, grade-level vocabulary in writing to clearly communicate complex ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses precise and advanced vocabulary to clearly explain complex ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use resources (e.g., dictionary, glossary, thesaurus, etc.) to determine or clarify the meanings, syllabication, pronunciation, synonyms, parts of speech, and etymology of words or phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use tools like dictionaries and thesauruses to understand word meanings, pronunciation, parts of speech, and word origins.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use word parts (e.g., affixes, Anglo-Saxon, Greek, and Latin roots, stems) to define and determine the meaning of increasingly complex words.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of complex words by looking at their parts, like prefixes, roots, and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context clues, connotation, and denotation to determine or clarify the meaning of words or distinguish among multiple-meaning words.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from sentences and word feelings to understand or choose the right meaning of words with multiple meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationships among synonyms, antonyms, and analogies.
description_plain: Your child understands how words with similar or opposite meanings and comparisons relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will expand and apply their spoken and reading vocabularies to speak, read, and write effectively.
description_plain: Your child can grow and use their speaking and reading vocabulary to talk, read, and write well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend narrative, informative, and argumentative writing to suit their audience and purpose.
description_plain: Your child can mix storytelling, informative facts, and opinions in writing to fit the audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose argumentative essays, reviews, or op-eds that:- introduce precise, informed claims- include a defensible thesis- acknowledge counterclaims or alternate perspectives- organize claims, counterclaims, and evidence in a logical sequence- provide the most relevant evidence to develop balanced arguments, using credible sources- use sentence variety and word choice to create clarity and concision- use style and tone that suits the audience and purpose
description_plain: Your child can write opinion essays or reviews that clearly state their claim, include opposing views, use strong evidence, and use clear and varied sentences for their audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose informative essays, reports, or technical writing that:- objectively introduce and develop topics- include a defensible thesis- incorporate evidence (e.g., specific facts, details, charts and graphs, data)- maintain an organized structure- use sentence variety and word choice to create clarity and concision- establish and maintain a formal style- emulate literary devices from mentor texts
description_plain: Your child can write reports or essays that explain topics clearly with facts, organize ideas well, and use formal language and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose narratives reflecting real or imagined experiences that:- include engaging plots involving well-developed, complex characters resolving conflicts- establish narrator(s) that enhance(s) the narrative- are intentionally sequenced in a way to achieve a specific effect (e.g., create suspense, establish mood, reflect theme)- provide clear descriptions, using precise language, sensory details, and dialogue- include varied syntax to enhance readability- emulate literary elements and/or literary devices from mentor texts
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events with interesting plots, detailed characters, clear descriptions, and dialogue to create mood or suspense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how two or more texts address similar themes or topics, using textual evidence to support their claims and inferences.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas and use examples from the texts to support their thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how informational text structures support the author's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the way an informational text is organized helps the author share their main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how authors writing on the same issue reached different conclusions because of differences in assumptions, evidence, reasoning, and viewpoints, including examining rhetorical appeals, bias, and use of logical fallacies.
description_plain: Your child can explain why different authors have different opinions on the same topic by looking at their evidence, reasons, and possible biases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how literary devices impact theme, mood, and/or tone, using textual evidence:- figurative language (i.e., simile, metaphor, personification, hyperbole, imagery, symbolism)- sound devices (i.e., onomatopoeia, alliteration, assonance)- irony (i.e., verbal, situational, dramatic)
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors use things like similes, metaphors, sounds, and irony to affect the story’s mood or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how literary elements impact theme, mood, and/or tone, using textual evidence:- setting- plot structure (e.g., foreshadowing, flashback, in media res)- conflict (i.e., internal, external)- characters (e.g., protagonist, antagonist)- characterization (i.e., direct, indirect)- point of view (e.g., narrator reliability)- archetypes
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story like setting, plot, characters, and point of view affect its mood and theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate authors' perspectives and explain how those perspectives contribute to the meanings of texts.
description_plain: Your child can understand an author’s point of view and explain how it shapes the meaning of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the extent to which historical, cultural, and/or global perspectives affect authors' stylistic choices in grade-level literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how history, culture, or world views influence how authors write stories or informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will apply critical thinking skills to reading and writing.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully about what they read and write to understand and explain ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively publish final drafts for an authentic audience (e.g., publishing digitally, community and professional audiences, newspapers and magazines, entering contests).
description_plain: Your child regularly shares their finished writing with real audiences, like online, in newspapers, or contests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively use resources to edit for grammar, usage, mechanics, and format in order to finalize drafts for publication.
description_plain: Your child regularly uses tools to check and fix grammar, spelling, punctuation, and formatting before sharing their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively revise drafts for organization, transitions, sentence variety, and consistent tone and point of view in order to improve coherence and meaning.
description_plain: Your child regularly revises their writing to improve organization, transitions, sentence variety, and consistent tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively develop drafts, applying organizational structure(s) (e.g., description, compare/contrast, sequential, problem/solution, cause/effect, etc.).
description_plain: Your child regularly develops drafts using different ways to organize ideas, like comparing, sequencing, or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely and recursively prewrite (e.g., develop and organize ideas).
description_plain: Your child regularly plans and organizes ideas before writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify characteristics of genres and analyze how they enhance comprehension of fiction, poetry, drama, and nonfiction.
description_plain: Your child can recognize different types of stories and texts and explain how their features help understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the main ideas and paraphrase significant parts of increasingly complex texts.
description_plain: Your child can summarize main ideas and restate important parts of harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will use a variety of recursive reading and writing processes.
description_plain: Your child uses different steps over and over to read and write better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct formal and informal presentations in a variety of contexts supporting their message with evidence and using verbal and nonverbal cues.
description_plain: Your child can give formal and informal presentations using evidence and clear speaking and body language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules as they engage in collaborative discussions about what they are reading and writing, expressing their own ideas clearly, building on the ideas of others, and respectfully disagreeing when necessary in pairs, diverse groups, and whole-class settings.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules in group discussions, share their ideas clearly, listen to others, and disagree respectfully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work effectively and respectfully in diverse groups by showing willingness to make necessary compromises to accomplish a goal, sharing responsibility for collaborative work, and recognizing individual contributions
description_plain: Your child can work well and respectfully in diverse groups by sharing responsibility and recognizing others’ contributions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively listen in order to analyze and evaluate speakers' verbal and nonverbal messages by asking questions to clarify purpose and perspective.
