description_plain: Your child listens carefully to understand speakers’ messages and asks questions to clarify their purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively listen using agreed-upon discussion rules with control of verbal and nonverbal cues.
description_plain: Your child listens actively and follows agreed rules using both words and body language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Students will listen and speak effectively in a variety of situations.
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and speak clearly in different situations.

subject: Math
description: Draw conclusions from real-object graphs and pictographs.
description_plain: Your child can look at real-object graphs and pictures and make conclusions from them.

subject: Math
description: Use categorical data to create real-object graphs and pictographs.
description_plain: Your child can use categories of data to make real-object graphs and picture graphs.

subject: Math
description: Collect and organize information about objects and events in the environment.
description_plain: Your child can collect and organize information about things and events around them.

subject: Math
description: Collect, organize, and interpret categorical data.
description_plain: Your child can collect, organize, and understand data sorted into categories.

subject: Math
description: Develop an awareness of simple time concepts within daily life, using age-appropriate vocabulary (e.g., yesterday, today, tomorrow, morning, afternoon, night).
description_plain: Your child learns simple time words like yesterday, today, and tomorrow and understands daily time concepts.

subject: Math
description: Tell time as it relates to daily life.
description_plain: Your child can tell time related to daily activities.

subject: Math
description: Compare the number of objects needed to fill two different containers.
description_plain: Your child can compare how many objects fit into two different containers.

subject: Math
description: Identify more than one shared attribute between objects, and sort objects into sets.
description_plain: Your child can find more than one thing that objects have in common and sort them into groups.

subject: Math
description: Order up to 6 objects using measurable attributes, such as length and weight.
description_plain: Your child can put up to six objects in order by size or weight.

subject: Math
description: Use words to compare objects according to length, size, weight, position, and location.
description_plain: Your child can use words to compare objects by length, size, weight, position, and location.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order objects according to location and measurable attributes.
description_plain: Your child can compare and put objects in order based on where they are and their size or weight.

subject: Math
description: Use basic shapes and spatial reasoning to represent objects in the real world.
description_plain: Your child can use simple shapes and spatial thinking to represent real-world objects.

subject: Math
description: Compose larger, undefined shapes and structures using three-dimensional objects.
description_plain: Your child can build bigger shapes and structures using 3D objects.

subject: Math
description: Use smaller two-dimensional shapes to fill in the outline of a larger two-dimensional shape.
description_plain: Your child can fill a big flat shape by fitting smaller flat shapes inside its outline.

subject: Math
description: Identify attributes of two-dimensional shapes using informal and formal geometric language interchangeably, such as the number of corners/vertices and the number of sides/edges.
description_plain: Your child can talk about flat shapes by counting their corners and sides using simple words.

subject: Math
description: Sort two-dimensional objects using characteristics such as shape and size.
description_plain: Your child can group flat shapes by their size and shape.

subject: Math
description: Recognize squares, circles, triangles, and rectangles.
description_plain: Your child can recognize squares, circles, triangles, and rectangles.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and sort basic two-dimensional shapes; use two-dimensional and three-dimensional shapes to represent real-world objects.
description_plain: Your child can find and sort basic flat shapes and use flat and solid shapes to show real things.

subject: Math
description: Recognize, duplicate, complete, and extend repeating, increasing, and decreasing patterns in a variety of contexts (i.e., shape, color, size, objects, sounds, movement).
description_plain: Your child can copy, finish, and make patterns that repeat, grow, or shrink using colors, shapes, sizes, sounds, or movements.

subject: Math
description: Sort and group up to 10 objects into a set based upon characteristics such as color, size, and shape. Explain verbally what the objects have in common.
description_plain: Your child can sort up to 10 objects by color, size, or shape and explain what they have in common.

subject: Math
description: Duplicate patterns in a variety of contexts.
description_plain: Your child can copy patterns in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Identify pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters by name.
description_plain: Your child can name pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters.

subject: Math
description: Identify coins by name.
description_plain: Identify coins by name.

subject: Math
description: Distribute a set of objects into at least two smaller equal sets.
description_plain: Your child can share a group of objects into at least two equal smaller groups.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between whole numbers and fractions through fair share.
description_plain: Your child understands that whole numbers can be split into equal parts like shares.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose numbers up to 10 using objects and pictures.
description_plain: Your child can make and break apart numbers up to 10 using objects and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Develop conceptual understanding with addition and subtraction (up to 10) using objects and pictures.
description_plain: Your child learns addition and subtraction up to 10 using objects and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order whole numbers from 0 to 10 with and without objects, using the vocabulary "more than," "less than," or "equal to."
description_plain: Your child can compare numbers from 0 to 10 and say which is more, less, or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Find a number that is 1 more or 1 less than a given number up to 10.
description_plain: Your child can find a number that is one more or one less than a given number up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, discuss, and represent whole numbers from 0 to at least 20. Representations may include numerals, pictures, real-object and pictographs, spoken words, and manipulatives.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, talk about, and show numbers from 0 to at least 20 using numbers, pictures, or objects.

subject: Math
description: Count forward, with and without objects, from any given number up to 20.
description_plain: Your child can count forward from any number up to 20, with or without objects.

subject: Math
description: Recognize without counting (subitize) the quantity of a small group of objects in organized and random arrangements up to 10.
description_plain: Your child can quickly see how many objects are in a small group up to 10 without counting.

subject: Math
description: Use ordinal numbers to represent the position of an object in a sequence up to 10.
description_plain: Your child can use words like first, second, and third to show the order of objects up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that a number can be used to represent how many objects are in a set up to 10.
description_plain: Your child understands that numbers tell how many objects are in a group up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Count aloud forward in sequence to 100 by 1s and 10s.
description_plain: Your child can count out loud by ones and tens up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between quantities and whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands how numbers relate to amounts of things.

subject: Math
description: Draw conclusions from pictographs and bar graphs.
description_plain: Your child can understand information shown in picture and bar graphs.

subject: Math
description: Use data to create pictographs and bar graphs that demonstrate one-to-one correspondence.
description_plain: Your child can make picture and bar graphs that match one picture or bar to one item.

subject: Math
description: Collect, sort, and organize data in up to three categories using representations (e.g., tally marks, tables, Venn diagrams).
description_plain: Your child can collect, sort, and organize data into up to three groups using marks, tables, or diagrams.

subject: Math
description: Collect, organize, and interpret categorical and numerical data.
description_plain: Your child can gather, organize, and understand different kinds of data.

subject: Math
description: Describe and measure calendar time by days, weeks, months, and years.
description_plain: Your child can describe and measure time using days, weeks, months, and years.

subject: Math
description: Tell time to the hour and half-hour (analog and digital).
description_plain: Your child can tell time to the hour and half-hour on clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Describe and measure concepts of time.
description_plain: Your child can describe and measure time in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Use standard and nonstandard tools to identify volume/capacity. Compare and sort containers that hold more, less, or the same amount.
description_plain: Your child can use tools to find how much containers hold and compare which holds more, less, or the same.

subject: Math
description: Describe a length to the nearest whole unit using a number with standard and nonstandard units.
description_plain: Your child can measure length to the nearest whole unit using both standard and nonstandard units.

subject: Math
description: Measure the same object/distance with units of two different lengths, and describe how and why the measurements differ.
description_plain: Your child can measure the same thing with two different units and explain why the numbers are different.

subject: Math
description: Illustrate that the length of an object is the number of same-size units of length that, when laid end-to-end with no gaps or overlaps, reach from one end of the object to the other.
description_plain: Your child understands that length is how many same-size units fit end-to-end along an object.

subject: Math
description: Use nonstandard and standard measuring tools to measure the length of objects.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long things are using both regular and everyday tools.

subject: Math
description: Select and use nonstandard and standard units to describe length and volume/capacity.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use different units to measure length and how much containers hold.

subject: Math
description: Recognize three-dimensional shapes such as cubes, cones, cylinders, pyramids, and spheres.
description_plain: Your child can recognize solid shapes like cubes, cones, cylinders, pyramids, and spheres.

subject: Math
description: Compose structures with three-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child can build structures using solid shapes.

subject: Math
description: Compose larger, defined shapes using smaller two-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child can make bigger flat shapes by putting smaller flat shapes together.

subject: Math
description: Identify regular and irregular trapezoids and hexagons by pointing to the shape when given the name.
description_plain: Your child can identify regular and irregular trapezoids and hexagons when named.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and compose two- and three-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and build with flat and solid shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify, create, complete, and extend repeating, increasing, and decreasing patterns in a variety of contexts (e.g., quantity, numbers, or shapes).
description_plain: Your child can find and make repeating or changing patterns using numbers, shapes, or amounts.

subject: Math
description: Identify patterns found in real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child understands how to spot patterns in everyday life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Determine the value of a collection of pennies, nickels, or dimes up to one dollar, counting by 1s, 5s, and 10s.
description_plain: Your child can count the value of pennies, nickels, and dimes up to one dollar by counting 1s, 5s, and 10s.

subject: Math
description: Write a number with the cent symbol to describe the value of a coin.
description_plain: Your child can write the value of a coin using numbers and the cent symbol (¢).

subject: Math
description: Identify pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters by name and value.
description_plain: Your child can name pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters and know how much each is worth.

subject: Math
description: Identify coins and their values.
description_plain: Your child understands different coins and how much money each one is worth.

subject: Math
description: Partition (fair share) sets of objects into two and three equal groups.
description_plain: Your child can divide groups of objects into two or three equal parts to share fairly.

subject: Math
description: Partition a regular polygon using physical models and recognize when those parts are equal.
description_plain: Your child can cut shapes into equal parts using models and tell when the parts are the same size.

subject: Math
description: Develop foundational ideas for fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands the basic idea of fractions as parts of a whole.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate fluency with basic facts of addition and subtraction with sums and minuends of up to 10.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true.
description_plain: Your child can tell if addition or subtraction equations with numbers up to 10 are correct.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems using addition and subtraction with sums and minuends of up to 10.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple addition and subtraction problems with numbers up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction problems with sums and minuends of up to 10 in real-world and mathematical contexts.
description_plain: Your child can use addition and subtraction to solve real-life and math problems with numbers up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Use words such as "more than," "less than," and "equal to" to describe the relative value of numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'more than,' 'less than,' and 'equal to' to compare numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use knowledge of number relationships to locate the position of a given whole number, up to 20, on an open number line.
description_plain: Your child can find where a number up to 20 belongs on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order whole numbers from 0 to 100.
description_plain: Your child can compare and put numbers from 0 to 100 in order.

subject: Math
description: Find a number that is 10 more or 10 less than a given number up to 100.
description_plain: Your child can find a number that is 10 more or 10 less than a number up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Count forward, without objects, by multiples of 1s, 2s, 5s, and 10s, up to 100.
description_plain: Your child can count forward by 1s, 2s, 5s, and 10s up to 100 without using objects.

subject: Math
description: Count forward, with objects, from any given number up to 100 by 1s, 2s, 5s and 10s.
description_plain: Your child can count forward by 1s, 2s, 5s, and 10s up to 100 using objects, starting from any number.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, discuss, and represent whole numbers up to 100. Representations may include numerals, words, addition and subtraction, pictures, tally marks, number lines, and manipulatives.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, talk about, and show numbers up to 100 in different ways like pictures, words, and number lines.

subject: Math
description: Use concrete representations to describe whole numbers between 10 and 100 in terms of tens and ones. Know that 10 is equivalent to 10 ones and 100 is equivalent to 10 tens.
description_plain: Your child can use objects to show numbers between 10 and 100 as groups of tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: Recognize numbers to 20 without counting (subitize) the quantity of structured arrangements.
description_plain: Your child can quickly recognize how many items are in a small group without counting.

subject: Math
description: Count, compare, and represent whole numbers up to 100, with an emphasis on grouping in terms of tens and ones.
description_plain: Your child can count, compare, and show numbers up to 100, focusing on groups of tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: Draw conclusions and make predictions from information in a pictograph and bar graph.
description_plain: Your child can look at pictographs and bar graphs to make guesses and conclusions about the data.

subject: Math
description: Write and solve one-step word problems involving addition or subtraction using data represented within pictographs and bar graphs with intervals of one.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve simple addition or subtraction word problems using information from pictographs and bar graphs.

subject: Math
description: Organize a collection of data with up to four categories using pictographs and bar graphs in intervals of 1s, 2s, 5s or 10s.
description_plain: Your child can organize data into up to four groups using pictographs and bar graphs with counting intervals like 1s, 2s, 5s, or 10s.

subject: Math
description: Explain that the length of a bar in a bar graph and the number of objects in a pictograph represents the number of data points for a given category.
description_plain: Your child understands that the length of bars in bar graphs and the number of pictures in pictographs show how many items are in each group.

subject: Math
description: Collect, organize, and interpret data.
description_plain: Your child can collect, organize, and understand information shown in graphs.

subject: Math
description: Read and write time to the quarter hour on an analog and digital clock.
description_plain: Your child can read and write the time to the nearest quarter hour on both analog and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish between a.m. and p.m.
description_plain: Your child knows the difference between a.m. (morning) and p.m. (afternoon/evening).

subject: Math
description: Tell time to the quarter hour.
description_plain: Your child can tell time to the nearest quarter hour.

subject: Math
description: Explore how varying shapes and styles of containers can have the same capacity.
description_plain: Your child explores how different shaped containers can hold the same amount.

subject: Math
description: Explain the relationship between length and the numbers on a ruler by using a ruler to measure lengths to the nearest whole unit.
description_plain: Your child can use a ruler to measure length to the nearest whole unit and explain how the numbers on the ruler show length.

subject: Math
description: Explain the relationship between the size of the unit of measurement and the number of units needed to measure the length of an object.
description_plain: Your child understands that smaller measuring units mean you need more of them to measure something longer.

subject: Math
description: Understand length as a measurable attribute and explore capacity.
description_plain: Your child learns how to measure length and explores how much containers can hold.

subject: Math
description: Recognize right angles and classify angles as smaller or larger than a right angle.
description_plain: Your child can recognize right angles and tell if other angles are bigger or smaller than a right angle.

subject: Math
description: Sort three-dimensional shapes based on attributes such as number of faces, vertices, and edges.
description_plain: Your child can sort 3D shapes by counting their faces, corners, and edges.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose two-dimensional shapes using triangles, squares, hexagons, trapezoids, and rhombi.
description_plain: Your child can put together and take apart flat shapes like triangles, squares, and hexagons.

subject: Math
description: Describe, compare, and classify two-dimensional figures according to their geometric attributes.
description_plain: Your child can describe, compare, and group flat shapes based on their features.

subject: Math
description: Recognize regular and irregular trapezoids and hexagons.
description_plain: Your child can recognize different kinds of trapezoids and hexagons, including regular and irregular ones.

subject: Math
description: Analyze attributes of two- and three-dimensional figures and develop generalizations about their properties.
description_plain: Your child can look at flat and solid shapes and notice what they have in common and how they are different.

subject: Math
description: Apply the commutative property, identity property, and number sense to find values for unknowns that make addition and subtraction number sentences true or false.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about adding and subtracting numbers to find missing numbers that make math sentences true or false.

subject: Math
description: Generate models and situations to represent number sentences and vice versa.
description_plain: Your child can create pictures or stories that show math sentences and can also write math sentences from those pictures or stories.

subject: Math
description: Use objects and number lines to represent number sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use objects and number lines to show and understand math sentences.

subject: Math
description: Use number sentences involving unknowns to represent and solve realworld and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve math problems that have missing numbers using number sentences.

subject: Math
description: Represent and describe repeating patterns involving shapes in a variety of contexts.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain repeating patterns made with shapes in different situations.

subject: Math
description: Represent, create, describe, complete, and extend increasing and decreasing patterns with quantity and numbers in a variety of contexts.
description_plain: Your child can make, describe, and continue patterns that go up or down using numbers and amounts.

subject: Math
description: Describe the relationship found in patterns to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child understands how patterns work and uses them to solve problems in real life and math.

subject: Math
description: Use a combination of coins to represent a given amount of money up to one dollar.
description_plain: Your child can use different coins to make a certain amount of money up to one dollar.

subject: Math
description: Determine the value of a collection of coins up to one dollar using the cent symbol.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how much a group of coins is worth, using the cent sign.

subject: Math
description: Determine the value of a set of coins.
description_plain: Your child can find the total value of a group of coins.

subject: Math
description: Construct equal-sized portions through fair sharing (length, set, and area models for halves, thirds, and fourths).
description_plain: Your child can divide things fairly into equal parts like halves, thirds, and fourths using objects, lengths, or areas.

subject: Math
description: Identify the parts of a set and area that represent fractions for halves, thirds, and fourths.
description_plain: Your child can point out parts of a group or shape that show halves, thirds, and fourths.

subject: Math
description: Explore the foundational ideas of fractions.
description_plain: Your child learns the basic ideas about fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use concrete models and structured arrangements, such as repeated addition, arrays, and ten frames to develop an understanding of multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can use objects and pictures like arrays and ten frames to understand multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction problems involving whole numbers up to two digits.
description_plain: Your child can solve addition and subtraction problems with numbers up to 99.

subject: Math
description: Use strategies and algorithms based on knowledge of place value and equality to add and subtract two-digit numbers.
description_plain: Your child uses place value and equality ideas to add and subtract two-digit numbers.

subject: Math
description: Estimate sums and differences up to 100.
description_plain: Your child can guess sums and differences of numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate fluency with basic facts of addition and subtraction with sums and minuends of up to 20.
description_plain: Your child knows addition and subtraction facts with numbers up to 20 quickly.

subject: Math
description: Use the relationship between addition and subtraction to generate basic facts with sums and minuends of up to 20.
description_plain: Your child uses the connection between adding and subtracting to figure out basic math facts up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract one- and two-digit numbers in real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract one- and two-digit numbers to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use place value to compare and order whole numbers up to 1,000 using comparative language, numbers, and symbols (e.g., 425 > 276, 73 < 107, page 351 comes after page 350, 753 is between 700 and 800).
description_plain: Your child can compare and put whole numbers up to 1,000 in order using words, numbers, and symbols like > and <.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round numbers to the nearest ten and nearest hundred (up to 1,000). Recognize when to round in real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can round numbers up to 1,000 to the nearest ten or hundred and knows when to do this in real life.

subject: Math
description: Use objects to determine whether a number is even or odd.
description_plain: Your child can use objects to tell if a number is even or odd.

subject: Math
description: Find 10 more or 10 less than a given three-digit number. Find 100 more or 100 less than a given three-digit number.
description_plain: Your child can find 10 more or less and 100 more or less than a three-digit number.

subject: Math
description: Use place value to describe whole numbers between 10 and 1,000 in terms of hundreds, tens, and ones, including written, standard, and expanded forms. Know that 10 is equivalent to 10 ones and 100 is equivalent to 10 tens.
description_plain: Your child can explain numbers between 10 and 1,000 by showing how many hundreds, tens, and ones they have in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Use knowledge of number relationships to locate the position of a given whole number, up to 100, on an open number line.
description_plain: Your child can find where a number up to 100 belongs on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, discuss, and represent whole numbers up to 1,000. Representations should include, but are not limited to, numerals, words, pictures, tally marks, number lines, and manipulatives.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, talk about, and show whole numbers up to 1,000 using numbers, words, pictures, tally marks, number lines, or objects.

subject: Math
description: Compare and represent whole numbers up to 1,000 with an emphasis on place value and equality.
description_plain: Your child can compare and show whole numbers up to 1,000 focusing on place value and equality.

subject: Math
description: Solve one- and two-step problems using categorical data represented with a frequency table, pictograph, or bar graph with scaled intervals.
description_plain: Your child can solve one- and two-step problems using data shown in tables, pictographs, or bar graphs.

subject: Math
description: Collect and organize a data set with multiple categories using a frequency table, line plot, pictograph, or bar graph with scaled intervals.
description_plain: Your child can collect and organize data with many categories using tables, line plots, pictographs, or bar graphs.

subject: Math
description: Collect, organize, and analyze data.
description_plain: Your child can collect, organize, and look at data to understand it.

subject: Math
description: Determine the solutions to problems involving addition and subtraction of time in intervals of five minutes, up to one hour, using pictorial models, number line diagrams, or other tools.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about adding and subtracting time in five-minute steps up to one hour using pictures or number lines.

subject: Math
description: Read and write time to the nearest five-minute interval (analog and digital).
description_plain: Your child can read and write time to the nearest five minutes on clocks and digital devices.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems by telling time to the nearest five-minute interval.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by telling time to the nearest five minutes.

subject: Math
description: Use an analog thermometer to determine temperature to the nearest degree in Fahrenheit and Celsius.
description_plain: Your child can use an analog thermometer to find the temperature in degrees Fahrenheit and Celsius.

subject: Math
description: Choose an appropriate measurement instrument and measure the length of objects to the nearest whole yard, whole foot, or half inch.
description_plain: Your child can pick the right tool and measure lengths to the nearest whole yard, foot, or half inch.

subject: Math
description: Choose an appropriate measurement instrument and measure the length of objects to the nearest whole centimeter or whole meter.
description_plain: Your child can pick the right tool and measure lengths to the nearest whole centimeter or meter.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of two-dimensional figures by counting the total number of same-size unit squares that fill the shape without gaps or overlaps
description_plain: Your child can find the area of flat shapes by counting how many same-size squares fit inside without gaps or overlaps.

subject: Math
description: Count cubes systematically to identify the number of cubes needed to pack the whole or half of a three-dimensional structure.
description_plain: Your child can count cubes carefully to find out how many are needed to fill all or half of a 3D shape.

subject: Math
description: Analyze why length and width are multiplied to find the area of a rectangle by decomposing the rectangle into one unit by one unit squares and viewing these as rows and columns to determine the area.
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying length and width finds the area of a rectangle by counting one-by-one squares arranged in rows and columns.

subject: Math
description: Find the perimeter of a polygon, given whole number lengths of the sides, using a variety of models.
description_plain: Your child can find the perimeter of shapes by adding the lengths of all sides using different tools or pictures.

subject: Math
description: Understand measurable attributes of real-world and mathematical objects using various tools.
description_plain: Your child understands how to measure real objects and shapes using rulers and other measuring tools.

subject: Math
description: Classify angles within a polygon as acute, right, obtuse, and straight.
description_plain: Your child can identify and name angles inside shapes as acute, right, obtuse, or straight.

subject: Math
description: Build a three-dimensional figure using unit cubes when shown a picture of a three-dimensional shape.
description_plain: Your child can build 3D shapes using small cubes when shown a picture of the shape.

subject: Math
description: Sort three-dimensional shapes based on attributes.
description_plain: Your child can group 3D shapes by their features like number of faces or edges.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and use geometric attributes to describe and create polygons and three-dimensional figures in various contexts.
description_plain: Your child can describe and make flat and 3D shapes by looking at their parts and properties.

subject: Math
description: Identify, represent, and apply the number properties (commutative, identity, and associative properties of addition and multiplication) using models and manipulatives to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use objects and pictures to show and use addition and multiplication rules to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use number sense with the properties of addition, subtraction, and multiplication, to find unknowns (represented by symbols) in one-step equations. Generate real-world situations to represent number sentences.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple math sentences with missing numbers and create real-life examples for these problems.

subject: Math
description: Use number sentences involving multiplication and unknowns to represent and solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication sentences with missing numbers to solve real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Explore and develop visual representations of increasing and decreasing geometric patterns and construct the next steps.
description_plain: Your child can look at growing or shrinking shape patterns and figure out what comes next.

subject: Math
description: Describe the rule (limited to a single operation) for a pattern from an input/output table or function machine involving addition, subtraction, or multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can find the rule for a pattern using addition, subtraction, or multiplication from a table or machine.

subject: Math
description: Create, describe, and extend patterns involving addition, subtraction, or multiplication to solve problems in a variety of contexts.
description_plain: Your child can make, describe, and continue patterns using addition, subtraction, or multiplication to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Describe and create representations of numerical and geometric patterns.
description_plain: Your child can describe and create number and shape patterns.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract a collection of bills up to twenty dollars using whole dollars in monetary transactions.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract bills up to twenty dollars using whole dollar amounts.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction to determine the value of a collection of coins up to one dollar using the cent symbol and in monetary transactions.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract coins up to one dollar and use the cent symbol when working with money.

subject: Math
description: Determine the value of a set of coins and determine the value of a set of bills in monetary transactions.
description_plain: Your child can find the total value of groups of coins and bills when buying or selling things.

subject: Math
description: Use models and number lines to order and compare fractions that are related to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and number lines to put fractions in order when they share the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Apply understanding of unit fractions and use this understanding to compose and decompose fractions related to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together fractions that come from the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Model fractions using length, set, and area for halves, thirds, fourths, sixths, and eighths.
description_plain: Your child can show fractions like halves, thirds, fourths, sixths, and eighths using lines, groups, or shaded areas.

subject: Math
description: Read and write fractions with words and symbols using appropriate terminology (i.e., numerator and denominator).
description_plain: Your child can read and write fractions using words and symbols, like numerator and denominator.

subject: Math
description: Use and justify fractional representations in real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use fractions to solve real-life and math problems and explain their answers.

subject: Math
description: Use various strategies (e.g., base ten blocks, area models, arrays, repeated addition, algorithms) based on knowledge of place value, equality, and properties of addition and multiplication to multiply a two-digit factor by a one-digit factor.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a two-digit number by a one-digit number using tools like blocks, arrays, or repeated addition.

subject: Math
description: Apply the relationship between multiplication and division to represent and solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about multiplication and division to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Represent division facts and divisibility by modeling a variety of approaches (e.g., repeated subtraction, equal sharing, forming equal groups) to show the relationship between multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can show division facts by using methods like sharing equally or subtracting repeatedly and explain how multiplication and division are connected.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction to solve problems involving whole numbers. Use various strategies, including the relationship between addition and subtraction and the context of the problem to assess the reasonableness of results.
description_plain: Your child can solve addition and subtraction problems using different strategies and check if their answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Recognize when to round numbers and apply understanding to estimate sums and differences to the nearest ten thousand, thousand, hundred, and ten.
description_plain: Your child knows when to round numbers and can estimate sums and differences to the nearest ten, hundred, thousand, or ten thousand.

subject: Math
description: Use strategies and algorithms based on knowledge of place value and equality to fluently add and subtract up to five-digit numbers (answer not to exceed 100,000).
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to five digits using place value and other methods.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate fluency with multiplication facts using factors up to 10.
description_plain: Your child knows multiplication facts for numbers up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Represent multiplication facts by modeling a variety of approaches (e.g., manipulatives, repeated addition, equal-sized groups, arrays, area models, equal jumps on a number line, skip counting).
description_plain: Your child can show multiplication facts using objects, repeated addition, groups, arrays, area models, number lines, or skip counting.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round numbers to the nearest thousand, ten-thousand and hundred thousand.
description_plain: Your child can round numbers to the nearest thousand, ten thousand, or hundred thousand using place value.

subject: Math
description: Use place value to compare and order whole numbers, up to 100,000, using comparative language, numbers, and symbols.
description_plain: Your child can compare and order whole numbers up to 100,000 using words, numbers, and symbols.

subject: Math
description: Applying knowledge of place values, use mental strategies (no written computations) to find 100 more or 100 less than a given number, 1,000 more or 1,000 less than a given number, and 10,000 more or 10,000 less than a given number, up to a five-digit number.
description_plain: Your child can mentally find 100, 1,000, or 10,000 more or less than a given number up to 99,999.

subject: Math
description: Use place value to describe whole numbers between 1,000 and 100,000 in terms of ten thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens and ones, including written, standard, and expanded forms.
description_plain: Your child can describe numbers between 1,000 and 100,000 by their ten thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones, and write them in different forms.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, discuss, and represent whole numbers up to 100,000. Representations should include but are not limited to numerals, words, pictures, number lines, and manipulatives (e.g., 350 = 3 hundreds, 5 tens = 35 tens = 3 hundreds, 4 tens, 10 ones).
description_plain: Your child can read, write, talk about, and show numbers up to 100,000 using numbers, words, pictures, number lines, and objects.

subject: Math
description: Compare and represent whole numbers up to 100,000 with an emphasis on place value and equality.
description_plain: Your child can compare and show whole numbers up to 100,000 focusing on place value and equal amounts.

subject: Math
description: Solve one- and two-step problems by analyzing data in whole number, decimal, or fraction form in a frequency table and line plot.
description_plain: Your child can solve one- and two-step problems by looking at data in tables and line plots with whole numbers, decimals, or fractions.

subject: Math
description: Organize data sets to create tables, bar graphs, timelines, and Venn diagrams. The data may include benchmark fractions or decimals (¼, 1/3, ½, 2/3, ¾, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75).
description_plain: Your child can organize data to make tables, bar graphs, timelines, and Venn diagrams using fractions or decimals like ¼, ½, or 0.25.

subject: Math
description: Create and organize data on a frequency table or line plot marked with whole numbers and fractions using appropriate titles, labels, and units.
description_plain: Your child can collect and organize data in tables or line plots with whole numbers and fractions, including titles, labels, and units.

subject: Math
description: Summarize, construct, and analyze data.
description_plain: Your child can collect, organize, and explain information from data.

subject: Math
description: Convert one measure of time to another including seconds to minutes, minutes to hours, hours to days, and vice versa, using various models.
description_plain: Your child can change units of time, like seconds to minutes or hours to days, using different tools.

subject: Math
description: Determine elapsed time.
description_plain: Determine elapsed time.

subject: Math
description: Determine elapsed time and convert between units of time.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how much time has passed and switch between different time units.

subject: Math
description: Determine and justify the best use of customary and metric measurements in a variety of situations (liquid volumes, mass vs. weight, temperatures above 0 (zero) degrees, and length).
description_plain: Your child can choose the best way to measure things like liquids, weight, temperature, and length using customary or metric units.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and use the relationship between millimeters, centimeters, and meters to measure and compare objects.
description_plain: Your child understands how millimeters, centimeters, and meters relate and can use them to measure and compare objects.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and use the relationship between inches, feet, and yards to measure and compare objects.
description_plain: Your child understands how inches, feet, and yards relate and can use them to measure and compare objects.

subject: Math
description: Choose an appropriate instrument to measure the length of an object to the nearest whole centimeter or quarter inch.
description_plain: Your child can pick the right tool to measure an object's length to the nearest whole centimeter or quarter inch.

subject: Math
description: Develop the concept that the volume of rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths can be found by counting the total number of same-sized unit cubes that fill a shape without gaps or overlaps. Use a variety of tools and create models to determine the volume using appropriate measurements (e.g., cm³).
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of box-shaped objects by counting how many small cubes fit inside and use tools to measure in cubic centimeters.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of polygons by determining if they can be decomposed into rectangles.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of shapes by breaking them into rectangles.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in geometric figures and real-world objects with a protractor or angle ruler.
description_plain: Your child can measure angles in shapes and real objects using a protractor or angle ruler.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and measure attributes in real-world and mathematical situations using various tools.
description_plain: Your child can measure different features of objects and shapes using various tools.

subject: Math
description: Given two three-dimensional shapes, identify each shape. Compare and contrast their similarities and differences based on their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can name two 3D shapes and explain how they are alike and different.

subject: Math
description: Describe, classify, and construct quadrilaterals, including squares, rectangles, trapezoids, rhombuses, parallelograms, and kites. Recognize quadrilaterals in various models.
description_plain: Your child can describe, sort, and build four-sided shapes like squares, rectangles, and trapezoids, and spot them in real life.

subject: Math
description: Identify points, lines, line segments, rays, angles, endpoints, and parallel and perpendicular lines in various models.
description_plain: Your child can find points, lines, line segments, rays, angles, and tell which lines are parallel or perpendicular in different examples.

subject: Math
description: Name, describe, classify, and construct polygons and three-dimensional figures based on their attributes; recognize polygons and three-dimensional figures in real-life and mathematical situations.
description_plain: Your child can name, describe, sort, and build flat shapes and 3D figures, and recognize them in everyday life and math.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown addend or factor in equivalent and non-equivalent expressions (e.g., 5 + 6 = 4 + __, 3 ∙ 8 < 3 ∙ __).
description_plain: Your child can find missing numbers in addition or multiplication problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve for a variable in an equation involving addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division with whole numbers. Analyze models to represent number sentences and vice versa.
description_plain: Your child can solve equations with addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division to find unknown numbers and use models to show the problems.

subject: Math
description: Use the relationships between multiplication and division with the properties of multiplication to solve problems and find values for variables that make number sentences true.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication and division facts to solve problems and find numbers that make equations true.

subject: Math
description: Use multiplication and division with variables to create number sentences representing a given mathematical situation.
description_plain: Your child can write multiplication and division sentences with unknown numbers to match a math situation.

subject: Math
description: Construct models to show growth patterns involving geometric shapes and define the single operation rule of the pattern.
description_plain: Your child can build models to show growing patterns with shapes and explain the rule that creates the pattern.

subject: Math
description: Describe the single operation rule for a pattern from an input/output table or function machine involving any operation of a whole number.
description_plain: Your child can explain the rule for a pattern using input/output tables or machines with whole number operations.

subject: Math
description: Create an input/output chart or table to represent or extend a numerical pattern.
description_plain: Your child can make charts or tables to show or continue number patterns.

subject: Math
description: Describe, create, and analyze multiple representations of patterns to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can describe, create, and study different kinds of patterns to solve problems in math and real life.

subject: Math
description: Given a total cost (dollars and coins up to twenty dollars) and amount paid (dollars and coins up to twenty dollars), find the change required in a variety of ways.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how much change to give back when paying for something costing up to twenty dollars.

subject: Math
description: Select the fewest number of coins for a given amount of money up to one dollar.
description_plain: Your child can choose the smallest number of coins to make amounts up to one dollar.

subject: Math
description: Determine the value of bills and coins in order to solve monetary transactions.
description_plain: Your child can count the value of bills and coins to solve money problems.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order benchmark fractions (0, ¼, 1/3, ½, 2/3, ¾, 1) and decimals (0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1.00) in a variety of representations.
description_plain: Your child can compare and put in order common fractions and decimals like ¼, ½, 0.25, and 0.75 using different ways to show them.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order decimals and whole numbers using place value and various models including but not limited to grids, number lines, and base 10 blocks.
description_plain: Your child can compare and order decimals and whole numbers using place value and tools like number lines and blocks.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals in standard, word, and expanded form up to at least the hundredths place in a variety of contexts, including money.
description_plain: Your child can read and write decimals up to hundredths in numbers, words, and expanded form, including money.

subject: Math
description: Represent tenths and hundredths with concrete and pictorial models, making connections between fractions and decimals.
description_plain: Your child can show tenths and hundredths using pictures and objects and understand how fractions and decimals connect.

subject: Math
description: Use models to add and subtract fractions with like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can use models to add and subtract fractions that have the same bottom number.

subject: Math
description: Decompose a fraction into a sum of fractions with the same denominator in more than one way, using concrete and pictorial models and recording results with numerical representations (e.g., ¾ = ¼ + ¼ + ¼ and ¾ = 2/4 + ¼).
description_plain: Your child can break a fraction into smaller parts in different ways using models and write the parts as numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use models to order and compare whole numbers and fractions less than and greater than one, using comparative language and symbols.
description_plain: Your child can use models to compare and order whole numbers and fractions, using words and symbols.

subject: Math
description: Use benchmark fractions (0, ¼. 1/3, ½, 2/3, ¾, 1) to locate additional fractions with denominators up to twelfths on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can use common fractions like ¼ and ½ to find other fractions on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Represent and rename equivalent fractions using fraction models (e.g., parts of a set, area models, fraction strips, number lines).
description_plain: Your child can show and rename equal fractions using models like fraction strips and number lines.

subject: Math
description: Represent and compare fractions and decimals in real-world and mathematical situations; use place value to understand decimal quantities.
description_plain: Your child can show and compare fractions and decimals in real life and math, and use place value to understand decimals.

subject: Math
description: Use strategies and algorithms (e.g., mental strategies, standard algorithms, partial quotients, repeated subtraction, the commutative, associative, and distributive properties) based on knowledge of place value, equality, and properties of operations to divide a 3-digit dividend by a 1-digit whole number divisor, with and without remainders.
description_plain: Your child can divide 3-digit numbers by 1-digit numbers using different methods, with or without leftovers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and analyze models to solve multi-step problems requiring the use of addition, subtraction, and multiplication of multi- digit whole numbers. Use various strategies, including the relationship between operations, the use of appropriate technology, and the context of the problem to assess the reasonableness of results.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step problems using addition, subtraction, and multiplication with large numbers, and check if answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Estimate products of 3-digit by 1-digit and 2-digit by 2-digit whole number factors using a variety of strategies (e.g., rounding, front end estimation, adjusting, compatible numbers) to assess the reasonableness of results. Explore larger numbers using technology to investigate patterns.
description_plain: Your child can estimate multiplication answers by rounding and other strategies to see if results are reasonable.

subject: Math
description: Multiply 3-digit by 1-digit and 2-digit by 2-digit whole numbers, using various strategies, including but not limited to standard algorithms.
description_plain: Your child can multiply 3-digit numbers by 1-digit numbers and 2-digit numbers by 2-digit numbers using different strategies.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate fluency with multiplication and division facts with factors up to 12.
description_plain: Your child can quickly recall multiplication and division facts for numbers up to 12.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems using multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can solve everyday and math problems using multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Use place value to compare and order whole numbers up to 1,000,000, using comparative language, numbers, and symbols.
description_plain: Your child can use place value to compare and put whole numbers up to 1,000,000 in order using words, numbers, and symbols.

subject: Math
description: Applying knowledge of place value, use mental strategies (no written computations) to multiply or divide a number by 10, 100 and 1,000.
description_plain: Your child can mentally multiply or divide numbers by 10, 100, or 1,000 without writing it down.

subject: Math
description: Use place value to describe whole numbers between 1,000 and 1,000,000 in terms of millions, hundred thousands, ten thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones with written, standard, and expanded forms.
description_plain: Your child can explain whole numbers between 1,000 and 1,000,000 by breaking them into millions, hundred thousands, ten thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones using different forms.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, discuss, and represent whole numbers up to 1,000,000. Representations may include numerals, words, pictures, number lines, and manipulatives.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, talk about, and show whole numbers up to 1,000,000 using numbers, words, pictures, number lines, or objects.

subject: Math
description: Compare and represent whole numbers up to 1,000,000 with an emphasis on place value and equality.
description_plain: Your child can compare and show whole numbers up to 1,000,000, focusing on place value and equality.

subject: Math
description: Create and analyze line and double-bar graphs with increments of whole numbers, fractions, and decimals.
description_plain: Your child can make and study line graphs and double-bar graphs using whole numbers, fractions, and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Find the measures of central tendency (i.e., mean, median, mode) and range of a set of data. Understand that the mean is a "leveling out" or central balance point of the data.
description_plain: Your child can find the average (mean), middle value (median), most common value (mode), and range of a set of data and understands that the mean balances the data.

subject: Math
description: Create and analyze data to find the range and measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode).
description_plain: Your child can collect and study data to find the range and averages like mean, median, and mode.

subject: Math
description: Estimate lengths and geometric measurements to the nearest whole unit, using benchmarks in customary and metric measurement systems.
description_plain: Your child can guess lengths and measurements to the nearest whole unit using common references in both customary and metric systems.

subject: Math
description: Apply the relationship between millimeters, centimeters, and meters to measure, convert, and compare objects to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use the relationships between millimeters, centimeters, and meters to measure, convert, and compare objects to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply the relationship between inches, feet, and yards to measure, convert, and compare objects to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use the relationships between inches, feet, and yards to measure, convert, and compare objects to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object to the nearest whole centimeter or up to 1/16 inch using an appropriate instrument.
description_plain: Your child can measure the length of objects to the nearest whole centimeter or up to 1/16 inch using the right tools.

subject: Math
description: Measure and compare angles according to size using various tools.
description_plain: Your child can measure and compare angles using different tools.

subject: Math
description: Understand angle, length, weight, and capacity as measurable attributes of real-world and mathematical objects, using various tools to measure them. Solve real-world problems of length.
description_plain: Your child understands how to measure angles, length, weight, and capacity using tools and can solve real-world length problems.

subject: Math
description: Estimate the perimeter of polygons and create arguments for reasonable perimeter values of shapes that may include curves.
description_plain: Your child can estimate the perimeter of shapes and explain why their estimates make sense, even for shapes with curves.

subject: Math
description: Determine the volume of rectangular prisms by the number of unit cubes (n) used to construct the shape and by the product of the dimensions of the prism a · b · c = n. Understand rectangular prisms of different dimensions (p, q, and r) can have the same volume if a · b · c = p · q · r = n
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of rectangular boxes by counting unit cubes or multiplying length × width × height and knows different sized boxes can have the same volume.

subject: Math
description: Determine volume using the object's dimensions. Compare and analyze rectangular prisms with equivalent volume to recognize their different dimensions.
description_plain: Your child can find volume using measurements and compare boxes with the same volume but different sizes.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and draw a net for a three-dimensional figure (cube, rectangular prism, pyramid).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and draw flat patterns (nets) that fold into 3D shapes like cubes, boxes, and pyramids.

subject: Math
description: Describe, identify, and classify three-dimensional figures (cubes, rectangular prisms, and pyramids) and their attributes (number of edges, faces, vertices, shapes of faces), given various mathematical models.
description_plain: Your child can describe, identify, and sort 3D shapes like cubes, boxes, and pyramids by their edges, faces, corners, and face shapes.

subject: Math
description: Describe, identify, classify, and construct triangles (equilateral, right, scalene, isosceles) by their attributes using various mathematical models.
description_plain: Your child can describe, identify, sort, and build triangles (like equilateral, right, scalene, and isosceles) by their features.

subject: Math
description: Describe, identify, classify, and construct two- and three-dimensional figures using their geometric attributes.
description_plain: Your child can describe, identify, sort, and build 2D and 3D shapes using their geometric features.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate expressions involving variables when values for the variables are given.
description_plain: Your child can solve math expressions with variables when given the values for those variables.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether an equation or inequality involving a variable is true or false for a given value of the variable.
description_plain: Your child can decide if an equation or inequality with a variable is true or false when given a value for the variable.

subject: Math
description: Generate equivalent numerical expressions and solve problems using number sense involving whole numbers by applying the commutative property, associative property, distributive property, and order of operations (excluding exponents).
description_plain: Your child can create equal math expressions and solve problems using properties like changing the order or grouping of numbers and following the correct steps.

subject: Math
description: Understand and interpret expressions, equations, and inequalities involving variables and whole numbers, and use them to represent and evaluate real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses expressions, equations, and inequalities with variables and whole numbers to solve real and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use a rule or table to represent ordered pairs of whole numbers and graph these ordered pairs on a coordinate plane, identifying the origin and axes in relation to the coordinates.
description_plain: Your child can use rules or tables to show pairs of whole numbers and plot them on a graph, knowing where the origin and axes are.

subject: Math
description: Use tables and rules with up to two operations to describe patterns of change and make predictions and generalizations about various mathematical situations.
description_plain: Your child can use tables and rules with up to two math operations to describe patterns and make predictions.

subject: Math
description: Describe and graph patterns of change created through numerical patterns.
description_plain: Your child can describe and graph patterns made by numbers changing in a certain way.

subject: Math
description: Apply mental math and knowledge of place value (no written computations) to find 0.1 more or 0.1 less than a number, 0.01 more or 0.01 less than a number, and 0.001 more or 0.001 less than a number.
description_plain: Your child can mentally find numbers that are 0.1, 0.01, or 0.001 more or less than a given number without writing it down.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with like and unlike denominators, mixed numbers, and decimals, involving money, measurement, geometry, and data. Use various models and efficient strategies, including but not limited to standard algorithms.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with same or different bottom numbers, mixed numbers, and decimals using money, measurement, shapes, and data with different strategies.

subject: Math
description: Illustrate addition and subtraction of fractions with like and unlike denominators, mixed numbers, and decimals using a variety of mathematical models (e.g., fraction strips, area models, number lines, fraction rods).
description_plain: Your child can show how to add and subtract fractions with same or different bottom numbers, mixed numbers, and decimals using tools like fraction strips, area models, and number lines.

subject: Math
description: Estimate sums and differences of fractions with like and unlike denominators, mixed numbers, and decimals to assess the reasonableness of the results.
description_plain: Your child can estimate sums and differences of fractions and decimals to check if answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with like and unlike denominators, mixed numbers, and decimals to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions and decimals to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct models to solve multi-digit whole number problems requiring addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division using various representations, including the inverse relationships between operations, the use of technology, and the context of the problem to assess the reasonableness of results.
description_plain: Your child can use models and different methods, including technology, to solve big number problems with addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division and check if answers are reasonable.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that remainders can be represented in a variety of ways, including a whole number, fraction, or decimal. Determine the most meaningful form of a remainder based on the context of the problem.
description_plain: Your child understands that remainders in division can be shown as whole numbers, fractions, or decimals and can choose the best way depending on the problem.

subject: Math
description: Divide multi-digit numbers, by one- and two-digit divisors, based on knowledge of place value, including but not limited to standard algorithms.
description_plain: Your child can divide big numbers by one- or two-digit numbers using place value and methods like standard algorithms.

subject: Math
description: Estimate solutions to division problems to assess the reasonableness of results.
description_plain: Your child can estimate answers to division problems to see if their solutions make sense.

subject: Math
description: Divide multi-digit numbers and solve real-world and mathematical problems using arithmetic.
description_plain: Your child can divide big numbers and solve real-life and math problems using division.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and generate equivalent terminating decimals, fractions, mixed numbers, and fractions in various models.
description_plain: Your child can find and create decimals, fractions, and mixed numbers that mean the same thing using different pictures or models.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order decimals and fractions, including mixed numbers and fractions less than one, and locate on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can compare and put decimals and fractions in order, including mixed numbers and fractions less than one, and place them on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and represent decimals using place value to describe decimal numbers including fractional numbers as small as thousandths and whole numbers up to seven digits.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and show decimals using place value, including numbers as small as thousandths and whole numbers up to seven digits.

subject: Math
description: Represent decimal fractions (e.g., 1/10, 1/100) using a variety of models (e.g., 10 by 10 grids, base-ten blocks, meter stick) and show the rational number relationships among fractions, decimals and whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use different tools like grids and blocks to show decimal fractions and explain how fractions, decimals, and whole numbers are connected.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, represent, and compare fractions and decimals; recognize and write equivalent fractions; convert between fractions and decimals; use fractions and decimals in real-world and mathematical situations.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, show, and compare fractions and decimals; find fractions that are equal; change fractions to decimals and back; and use them to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate simple experiments in which the probabilities are known and compare the resulting relative frequencies with the known probabilities, recognizing that there may be differences between the two results.
description_plain: Your child can do simple probability experiments, compare the results to expected chances, and understand why the results might be different.

subject: Math
description: Determine the sample space for a given experiment and determine which members of the sample space are related to certain events. Sample space may be determined by the use of tree diagrams, tables or pictorial representations.
description_plain: Your child can list all possible outcomes of an experiment and figure out which outcomes match certain events using charts, trees, or pictures.

subject: Math
description: Represent possible outcomes using a probability continuum from impossible to certain.
description_plain: Your child can show possible outcomes on a scale from impossible to certain.

subject: Math
description: Use probability to model and solve mathematical problems; represent probabilities using fractions and decimals.
description_plain: Your child can use probability to solve math problems and show probabilities as fractions and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Explain and justify which measure of center (mean, median, or mode) would provide the most descriptive information for a given set of data.
description_plain: Your child can explain which average (mean, median, or mode) best describes a set of data and why.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the mean, median, and mode for a set of data.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain the mean, median, and mode of a group of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and analyze data.
description_plain: Interpret and analyze data.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems that require the conversion of lengths within the same measurement systems using appropriate units.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by changing lengths from one unit to another within the same measurement system.

subject: Math
description: Estimate weights and capacities using benchmarks in customary and metric measurement systems with appropriate units.
description_plain: Your child can guess weights and amounts using common examples in both customary and metric units.

subject: Math
description: Choose appropriate units of measurement and use ratios to convert within measurement systems to model and solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can pick the right units and use ratios to change measurements within the same system to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Develop and use the fact that the sum of the interior angles of a triangle is 180̊ to determine missing angle measures in a triangle.
description_plain: Your child can use the fact that the angles inside a triangle add up to 180 degrees to find missing angles.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems using the relationships between the angles (vertical, complementary, and supplementary) formed by intersecting lines.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using special angle relationships like vertical, complementary, and supplementary angles made by crossing lines.

subject: Math
description: Understand and use relationships between angles in geometric figures.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses angle relationships in shapes to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special quadrilaterals, and polygons that can be decomposed into triangles and other shapes.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special four-sided shapes, and polygons by breaking them into triangles and other shapes.

subject: Math
description: Develop and use formulas to determine the area of triangles and find unknown measures.
description_plain: Your child can create and use formulas to find the area of triangles and figure out missing measurements.

subject: Math
description: Develop and use formulas for the area of squares and parallelograms using a variety of methods including but not limited to the standard algorithms and finding unknown measures.
description_plain: Your child can create and use formulas to find the area of squares and parallelograms using different methods and find missing measurements.

subject: Math
description: Use mathematical modeling to calculate the area of squares, parallelograms, and triangles to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use math models to calculate the area of squares, parallelograms, and triangles to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Identify and describe the line(s) of symmetry in two-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child can find and describe lines of symmetry in flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that translations, reflections, and rotations preserve congruence and use them to show that two figures are congruent.
description_plain: Your child knows that sliding, flipping, and turning shapes keep their size and shape the same and can use these to show two shapes are the same.

subject: Math
description: Predict, describe, and apply translations (slides), reflections (flips), and rotations (turns) to a two-dimensional figure.
description_plain: Your child can predict, describe, and use slides, flips, and turns on flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Use translations, reflections, and rotations to establish congruence and understand symmetry (not on a coordinate plane).
description_plain: Your child can use slides, flips, and turns to understand when shapes are the same and to see symmetry without using a coordinate grid.

subject: Math
description: Use number sense and properties of operations and equality to model and solve mathematical problems involving equations in the form x + p = q and px = q, where p and q are nonnegative rational numbers. Graph the solution on a number line, interpret the solution in the original context, and assess the reasonableness of the solution.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple equations like x + p = q and px = q using number sense, show the answer on a number line, explain what it means, and check if it makes sense.

subject: Math
description: Model mathematical situations using expressions, equations and inequalities involving variables and rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use expressions, equations, and inequalities with variables and rational numbers to model math situations.

subject: Math
description: Use equations and inequalities to model and solve mathematical problems and use the idea of maintaining equality to solve equations. Interpret solutions in the original context.
description_plain: Your child can use equations and inequalities to solve math problems, keep both sides equal when solving, and explain the answers in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Generate equivalent expressions and evaluate expressions involving positive rational numbers by applying the commutative, associative, and distributive properties and order of operations to model and solve mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can create equal expressions and solve them by using math properties and the order of operations with positive rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use properties of arithmetic to generate equivalent numerical expressions and evaluate expressions involving positive rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to make equal number expressions and solve them with positive rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use and evaluate variables in expressions, equations, and inequalities that arise from various contexts, including determining when or if, for a given value of the variable, an equation or inequality involving a variable is true or false.
description_plain: Your child can use and check variables in expressions, equations, and inequalities from different situations to see if they are true or false for certain values.

subject: Math
description: Represent relationships between two varying positive quantities involving no more than two operations with rules, graphs, and tables; translate between any two of these representations.
description_plain: Your child can show how two changing positive quantities relate using rules, graphs, and tables, and switch between these ways.

subject: Math
description: Plot integer- and rational-valued (limited to halves and fourths) ordered-pairs as coordinates in all four quadrants and recognize the reflective relationships among coordinates that differ only by their signs.
description_plain: Your child can plot points with whole numbers and simple fractions in all four parts of a grid and understand how points reflect across axes.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent relationships between varying quantities; translate from one representation to another; use patterns, tables, graphs, and rules to model and solve mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and show how quantities change, switch between patterns, tables, graphs, and rules, and use these to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use mathematical modeling to solve and interpret problems including money, measurement, geometry, and data requiring arithmetic with decimals, fractions and mixed numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use math models to solve and understand problems with money, measurement, shapes, and data that need adding, subtracting, multiplying, or dividing decimals, fractions, and mixed numbers.

subject: Math
