description: Multiply and divide fractions and decimals using efficient and generalizable procedures.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions and decimals using easy and general methods.

subject: Math
description: Illustrate multiplication and division of fractions and decimals to show connections to fractions, whole number multiplication, and inverse relationships.
description_plain: Your child can show how multiplying and dividing fractions and decimals connect to whole number multiplication and how they undo each other.

subject: Math
description: Estimate solutions to problems with whole numbers, decimals, fractions, and mixed numbers, and use the estimates to assess the reasonableness of results in the context of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can estimate answers to problems with whole numbers, decimals, fractions, and mixed numbers and use these estimates to check if answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide decimals, fractions, and mixed numbers; solve real-world and mathematical problems with rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide decimals, fractions, and mixed numbers and solve real-life and math problems using these numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply the relationship between ratios, equivalent fractions, unit rates, and percents to solve problems in various contexts.
description_plain: Your child can use the connections between ratios, equal fractions, unit rates, and percents to solve different problems.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unit rate for ratios.
description_plain: Your child can find the unit rate when comparing two quantities using ratios.

subject: Math
description: Identify and use ratios to compare and relate quantities in multiple ways. Recognize that multiplicative comparison and additive comparison are different.
description_plain: Your child can use ratios to compare amounts in different ways and understands that multiplying and adding comparisons are not the same.

subject: Math
description: Explain and use the concept of ratio and its relationship to other rational numbers and to the multiplication and division of whole numbers. Use ratios to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child understands what ratios are, how they relate to fractions and multiplication, and uses them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Determine the greatest common factors and least common multiples. Use common factors and multiples to calculate with fractions, find equivalent fractions, and express the sum of two-digit numbers with a common factor using the distributive property.
description_plain: Your child can find the greatest common factor and least common multiple to work with fractions and simplify addition problems.

subject: Math
description: Factor whole numbers and express prime and composite numbers as a product of prime factors with exponents.
description_plain: Your child can break down whole numbers into prime factors and write them using exponents.

subject: Math
description: Identify and represent patterns with whole-number exponents and perfect squares. Evaluate powers with whole-number bases and exponents.
description_plain: Your child can recognize patterns with exponents and perfect squares and calculate powers with whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract integers in a variety of situations; use efficient and generalizable procedures including but not limited to standard algorithms.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract positive and negative numbers using different methods, including standard steps.

subject: Math
description: Illustrate addition and subtraction of integers using a variety of representations.
description_plain: Your child can show adding and subtracting positive and negative numbers using pictures, number lines, or other tools.

subject: Math
description: Estimate solutions for integer addition and subtraction of problems in order to assess the reasonableness of results.
description_plain: Your child can estimate answers when adding or subtracting whole numbers to check if their solutions make sense.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and model whole number and integer operations to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and use whole numbers and integers to solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Determine equivalencies among fractions, mixed numbers, decimals, and percents.
description_plain: Your child can find when fractions, mixed numbers, decimals, and percents are equal.

subject: Math
description: Explain that a percent represents parts "out of 100" and ratios "to 100."
description_plain: Your child understands that a percent means parts out of 100 or a ratio compared to 100.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order positive rational numbers, represented in various forms, or integers using the symbols "<", ">", and "=".
description_plain: Your child can compare and put in order positive fractions, decimals, and whole numbers using <, >, and = signs.

subject: Math
description: Use manipulatives and models (e.g., number lines) to determine positive and negative numbers and their contexts, identify opposites, and explain the meaning of 0 (zero) in a variety of situations.
description_plain: Your child can use objects and number lines to understand positive and negative numbers, their opposites, and what zero means.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and represent rational numbers expressed as integers, fractions, decimals, percents, and ratios; use these representations in real-world and mathematical situations.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and use numbers shown as integers, fractions, decimals, percents, and ratios in real life and math.

subject: Math
description: Use proportional reasoning to draw conclusions about and predict relative frequencies of outcomes based on theoretical probabilities.
description_plain: Your child can use proportional thinking to predict how often things will happen based on chance.

subject: Math
description: Calculate probability as a fraction of sample space or as a fraction of area. Express probabilities as percents, decimals and fractions.
description_plain: Your child can find probability as a fraction of all possible outcomes or area and show it as a fraction, decimal, or percent.

subject: Math
description: Determine the theoretical probability of an event using the ratio between the size of the event and the size of the sample space; represent probabilities as percents, fractions and decimals between 0 and 1.
description_plain: Your child can find the chance of an event by comparing the event size to all possible outcomes and show it as a percent, fraction, or decimal.

subject: Math
description: Calculate and use proportional reasoning with probabilities to model and solve mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can calculate and use ratios to solve problems involving probability.

subject: Math
description: Use technology to create and analyze box plots.
description_plain: Your child can use computers or calculators to make and study box plots.

subject: Math
description: Use reasoning with proportions to display and interpret data in circle graphs (pie charts) and histograms.
description_plain: Your child can use ratios to create and understand pie charts and histograms.

subject: Math
description: Design simple experiments, collect data, and calculate measures of center (mean, median, and mode) and spread (range and interquartile range). Use these quantities to draw conclusions about the data collected and make predictions.
description_plain: Your child can plan simple experiments, collect data, find averages and ranges, and use these to understand and predict results.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and analyze data, creating the most appropriate display, using a variety of tools.
description_plain: Your child can read and study data, choosing the best way to show it using different tools.

subject: Math
description: Graph and describe translations (with directional and algebraic instructions), reflections across the x- and y-axes, and rotations in 90̊ increments about the origin of figures on a coordinate plane, and determine the coordinates of the vertices of a figure after a transformation.
description_plain: Your child can move, flip, and turn shapes on a graph, describe these changes, and find new corner points.

subject: Math
description: Apply proportions, ratios, and scale factors to solve problems involving scale drawings and to determine side lengths and areas of similar triangles and rectangles.
description_plain: Your child can use ratios and scale to solve problems with scale drawings and find side lengths and areas of similar shapes.

subject: Math
description: Describe the properties of similarity, compare geometric figures for similarity, and determine scale factors resulting from dilations.
description_plain: Your child can describe when shapes are similar, compare them, and find scale changes from resizing.

subject: Math
description: Analyze the effect of translations, reflections, rotations, and dilations on the attributes of two-dimensional figures on and off the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can study how moving, flipping, turning, and resizing shapes changes their size and position.

subject: Math
description: Calculate the circumference and area of circles to solve problems in various contexts, in terms of pi (π) and using approximations for pi (π).
description_plain: Your child can find the distance around and area inside circles using pi or its approximations.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate an understanding of the proportional relationship between the diameter and circumference of a circle and that the unit rate (constant of proportionality) is pi (π) and can be approximated by rational numbers such as 22/7 and 3.14.
description_plain: Your child understands that the ratio of a circle’s circumference to its diameter is pi and can use numbers like 3.14 or 22/7 to estimate it.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems that require the conversion of weights and capacities within the same measurement systems using appropriate units.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by converting weights and volumes within the same measurement system.

subject: Math
description: Use mathematical models and reasoning with proportions and ratios to determine measurements, justify formulas, and solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use ratios and models to find measurements, explain formulas, and solve related problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area and perimeter of composite figures.
description_plain: Your child can find the area and perimeter of shapes made by combining other shapes.

subject: Math
description: Develop and use the formula to determine the area of a trapezoid.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use the formula to find the area of a trapezoid.

subject: Math
description: Use mathematical models and problems to calculate and justify the area of trapezoids and the area and perimeter of composite figures with rational measurements.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems and explain how to find the area of trapezoids and combined shapes.

subject: Math
description: Using a variety of tools and strategies, develop the concept that the volume of rectangular prisms can be found by counting the total number of same-sized unit cubes that fill a shape without gaps or overlaps. Use appropriate measurements (e.g., cm³).
description_plain: Your child can understand volume by counting how many small cubes fill a rectangular box and use correct units like cubic centimeters.

subject: Math
description: Using a variety of tools and strategies, develop the concept that surface area of a rectangular prism can be found by wrapping the figure with same-sized square units without gaps or overlap. Use appropriate measurements (e.g., cm²).
description_plain: Your child can find surface area by covering a rectangular box with squares without gaps and use units like square centimeters.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that the surface area of a rectangular prism can be found by finding the area of each component of the net of that figure. Know that rectangular prisms of different dimensions can have the same surface area.
description_plain: Your child knows that surface area comes from adding areas of all sides of a rectangular box and that boxes with different sizes can have the same surface area.

subject: Math
description: Develop and understand the concept of surface area and volume of rectangular prisms with rational-valued edge lengths.
description_plain: Your child learns about and understands surface area and volume of rectangular boxes with decimal edge lengths.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate numerical expressions using calculators and other technologies and justify solutions using order of operations and grouping symbols.
description_plain: Your child can use calculators to solve number problems and explain answers by following the correct order of steps.

subject: Math
description: Use properties of operations (associative, commutative, and distributive) to generate equivalent numerical and algebraic expressions containing rational numbers, grouping symbols and whole number exponents.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to rewrite number and letter expressions with fractions, grouping symbols, and exponents in different but equal ways.

subject: Math
description: Use order of operations and properties of operations to generate and evaluate equivalent numerical and algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use the correct order of steps and math rules to create and solve number and letter math expressions that mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Represent, write, solve, and graph problems leading to linear inequalities with one variable in the form x + p > q and x + p < q, where p, and q are nonnegative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write, solve, and graph math problems with inequalities like x + p > q or x + p < q, where p and q are positive numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write and solve problems leading to linear equations with one variable in the form px + q = r and p(x + q) = r, where p, q, and r are rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve math problems with equations like px + q = r or p(x + q) = r, where p, q, and r are numbers.

subject: Math
description: Represent mathematical situations using equations and inequalities involving variables and rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can show math situations using equations and inequalities with letters and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use proportional reasoning to assess the reasonableness of solutions.
description_plain: Your child can use proportional thinking to check if answers to problems make sense.

subject: Math
description: Use proportional reasoning to solve problems involving ratios.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that involve ratios by using proportional reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step problems with proportional relationships (e.g., distance-time, percent increase or decrease, discounts, tips, unit pricing, mixtures and concentrations, similar figures, other mathematical situations).
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step problems that involve proportional relationships like speed, discounts, tips, and mixtures.

subject: Math
description: Represent proportional relationships with tables, verbal descriptions, symbols, and graphs; translate from one representation to another. Determine and compare the unit rate (constant of proportionality, slope, or rate of change) given any of these representations.
description_plain: Your child can show proportional relationships using tables, words, symbols, and graphs, and find and compare unit rates from these.

subject: Math
description: Identify and justify proportional relationships using mathematical models and situations; solve problems involving proportional relationships and interpret results in the original context.
description_plain: Your child can identify proportional relationships in math problems, explain why they are proportional, solve related problems, and understand the answers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that the graph of a proportional relationship is a line through the origin and the coordinate (1, r), where r is the slope and the unit rate (constant of proportionality, k).
description_plain: Your child understands that graphs of proportional relationships are straight lines through zero and the point (1, r), where r is the unit rate.

subject: Math
description: Identify a relationship between two varying quantities, x and y, as proportional if it can be expressed in the form y/x = k or y = kx; distinguish proportional relationships from non-proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child can tell if two quantities are proportional by checking if their ratio is always the same and can tell proportional from non-proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Explain the concept of proportionality in mathematical models and situations and distinguish between proportional and non-proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child understands what proportionality means in math and can tell the difference between proportional and non-proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Model and solve problems using rational numbers involving addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and positive integer exponents.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using rational numbers with addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and whole number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Raise rational numbers (integers, fractions, and decimals) to positive integer exponents.
description_plain: Your child can raise rational numbers like integers, fractions, and decimals to whole number powers.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide integers in a variety of situations; use efficient and generalizable procedures, including standard algorithms.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide whole numbers in many situations using efficient methods.

subject: Math
description: Illustrate multiplication and division of integers using a variety of representations.
description_plain: Your child can show multiplication and division of whole numbers using different ways like pictures or models.

subject: Math
description: Estimate solutions to multiplication and division of integers in order to assess the reasonableness of results.
description_plain: Your child can estimate answers to multiplication and division problems with whole numbers to check if results are reasonable.

subject: Math
description: Calculate with rational numbers, with and without positive integer exponents, to model and solve mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can calculate with rational numbers, including using whole number exponents, to solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Explain the relationship between the absolute value of a rational number and the distance of that number from zero on a number line. Use the symbol for absolute value. Apply the concept of absolute value to model and solve problems.
description_plain: Your child understands that absolute value means how far a number is from zero on a number line and can use this idea to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and generate equivalent representations of rational numbers, including equivalent fractions.
description_plain: Your child can find and create different but equal forms of rational numbers, like equivalent fractions.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order rational numbers expressed in various forms using the symbols "<", ">", and "=".
description_plain: Your child can compare and order rational numbers written in different ways using <, >, and = symbols.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, represent, and compare rational numbers, expressed as integers, fractions, and decimals. Explain and apply the concept of absolute value.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, show, and compare rational numbers as whole numbers, fractions, and decimals, and understand absolute value.

subject: Math
description: Define, compare, and contrast the probabilities of dependent and independent events.
description_plain: Your child can explain and compare the chances of events that depend on each other and those that don't.

subject: Math
description: Determine how samples are chosen (randomness) to draw and support conclusions about generalizing a sample to a population, including identifying limitations and biases.
description_plain: Your child understands how random samples are chosen and can explain how this affects conclusions about a whole group.

subject: Math
description: Calculate experimental probabilities and represent them as percents, fractions, and decimals between 0 and 1. Use experimental probabilities to predict relative frequencies when actual probabilities are unknown.
description_plain: Your child can find experimental probabilities and show them as percents, fractions, or decimals, and use them to predict outcomes.

subject: Math
description: Calculate experimental probabilities and reason about probabilities to model and solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can calculate experimental probabilities and use them to solve problems about chance.

subject: Math
description: Collect, display, and interpret data using scatter plots. Use the shape of the scatter plot to find the informal line of best fit, make statements about the average rate of change, and make predictions about values not in the original data set. Use appropriate titles, labels, and units.
description_plain: Your child can collect and show data with scatter plots, find a line that fits the data, describe how fast things change on average, and predict values not in the data.

subject: Math
description: Explain how outliers affect measures of center and spread.
description_plain: Your child can explain how unusual data points affect the average and spread of data.

subject: Math
description: Describe the impact that inserting or deleting a data point has on the mean and the median of a data set. Create data displays using technology to examine this impact.
description_plain: Your child can describe how adding or removing a data point changes the mean and median, and use technology to show this.

subject: Math
description: Display and interpret data in a variety of ways, including using scatter plots and approximate lines of best fit. Use the line of best fit and average rate of change to make predictions and draw conclusions about data.
description_plain: Your child can show and understand data in different ways, including scatter plots and lines that best fit the data, to make predictions and conclusions.

subject: Math
description: Develop and use the formulas V = (πr)<sup></sup>h and V = Bh to determine the volume of right cylinders, in terms of π and using approximations for pi (Τ). Justify why base area (B) and height (h) are multiplied to find the volume of a right cylinder. Use appropriate units (e.g., cm³).
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of right cylinders, explain why multiplying base area and height works, and use correct units like cubic centimeters.

subject: Math
description: Justify why base area (B) and height (h) in the formula V=Bh are multiplied to find the volume of a rectangular prism. Use appropriate units (e.g., cm³).
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying base area and height finds the volume of rectangular prisms and use correct units like cubic centimeters.

subject: Math
description: Calculate the surface area of a cylinder, in terms of pi (Π) and using approximations for pi (Π), using decomposition or nets. Use appropriate units (e.g., cm²).
description_plain: Your child can find the surface area of cylinders using formulas with pi, breaking shapes into parts or nets, and use correct units like square centimeters.

subject: Math
description: Calculate the surface area of a rectangular prism using decomposition or nets. Use appropriate units (e.g., cm²).
description_plain: Your child can find the surface area of rectangular prisms using nets or breaking shapes into parts, and use correct units like square centimeters.

subject: Math
description: Justify and use formulas to calculate surface area and volume of three-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can explain and use formulas to find surface area and volume of 3D shapes.

description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean theorem to find the distance between two points on a graph.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can explain the Pythagorean theorem using measurements or pictures and use it to solve problems with right triangles.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean theorem to solve problems involving triangles.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean theorem to solve problems about triangles.

subject: Math
description: Represent real-world situations using equations and inequalities involving one variable.
description_plain: Your child can write equations and inequalities with one variable to represent real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Represent, write, solve, and graph problems leading to linear inequalities with one variable in the form px + q > r and px + q < r, where p, q, and r are rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write, solve, and graph problems with linear inequalities like px + q > r or px + q < r, where p, q, and r are numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems using linear equations with one variable where there could be one, infinitely many, or no solutions. Represent situations using linear equations and interpret solutions in the original context.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using simple equations with one variable and understand what the solutions mean in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems using mathematical models and situations with equations and inequalities involving linear expressions.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can explain each step when simplifying expressions by combining terms and following the correct order of operations, and can name the math rules used.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Represent linear functions with tables, verbal descriptions, symbols, and graphs; translate from one representation to another.

subject: Math
description: Identify and justify linear functions using mathematical models and situations; solve problems involving linear functions and interpret results in the original context.

subject: Math
